questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Main axis of a diatomic molecule is z, orbital px and py overlap with each other to form which of the following molecular orbitals?
questions.explanation: No overlapping. Overlapping will only possible when lobes are symmetrical about the axis perpendicular to the axis.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: N2 and O2 are converted into monoanions N2- and O2- respectively, which of the following statements is wrong?

options.C: In N2- , the N-N bond weakens
options.D: In O2- , the O-O bond order decreases

questions.explanation: O2 = π*₂ₚₓ1 = π*₂ₚᵧ1, paramagnetic. N2 = π₂ₚₓ2 = π₂ₚᵧ2, diamagnetic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following species does not exist under normal conditions?
questions.explanation: Be2 does not exist because Be2 = 8 = σ₁ₛ2,σ₂ₛ2

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecule stabilizes by removal of electron?
questions.explanation: On removing e- from O2 its bond order increases from 2 to 2.5. stability increases. Bond order O2 = 2, O2+ = 2.5

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Number of nodal planes in π₂ₚₓ are
questions.explanation: π orbitals have two nodal planes (one containg the internuclear axis and one perpendicular to it).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The low density of ice compared to water is due to
questions.explanation: H2O have more density because on increases, the temperature 'H' bonding interaction increases volume decreases and density increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following has the strongest O - O bond?
questions.explanation: More the bond order more will the bond strength. B.O O2+ = 2.5, O2 = 2, O2- = 1.5, O22- = 1. O2+ have maximum bond order and have strongest O - O bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which species does not exhibit paramagnetism?
questions.explanation: CO have 14 e- i.e. even no. and also have filled molecular orbitals. All paired e-.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: All have ionic, covalent and co-ordinate covalent bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: Full filled and half filled have maximum covalent character. ZnCl2 have 3d10 configuration have maximum polarising power.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In PO43- ion, the effective charge on each oxygen atom and P- O bond order respectively are
questions.explanation: Due to resonance, bond order is 1.25 and effective charge is -0.75 on each O.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A certain diatomic molecule, AB has dipole moment 1.6 D and the internuclear distance is 100 pm. The percentage of electronic charge existing on more electronegative atom is

questions.question: Dipole moment of is 1.1 D hence dipole moment of given compound will be
questions.explanation: dipole moment of 1, 3, 5 chlorine will be cancelled due to symmetry : dipole moment exist only due to C-2. i.e. 1.1 D

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the given structure of a compound, the correct various bond moments direction involving atoms are shown as

options.A: Br ← N ← CH2 → SiH2 ← CH2 → O ← CH3
options.B: Br → N → CH2 ← SiH2 → CH2 ← O → CH3
options.C: Br ← N ← CH2 → SiH2 ← CH2 → O ← CH3
options.D: Br → N ← CH2 → SiH2 ← CH2 → O ← CH3

questions.explanation: Electronegativity N > Br > C, Si < C, O > C. The arrows point from less electronegative to more electronegative.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which molecule contains both polar and non- polar covalent bond?
questions.explanation: H2O2 have open book structure. O-O bond is non-polar, O-H bonds are polar.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: CH2Cl2 is asymmetric, hence polar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is correct for XeO2F2 and PCl5 ?

options.A: Both have same hybridisation and shape
options.B: Both have same hybridisation but different geometry
options.C: Both have different hybridisation but same shape
options.D: Both have same hybridisation but different shape

questions.explanation: XeO2F2: H = 1/2 = 10/2 = 5 , see-saw shape. PCl5: H = 1/2 = 10/2 = 5 , trigonal bipyramidal shape.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: sp3d2 (octahedral) has 3 axes, so 3 angles of 180°. sp3d (trigonal bipyramidal) has only one axis (the axial one) with 180°.

questions.question: Incorrect statement regarding hybridization is

options.B: Number of hybrid orbital formed is same as the number of orbitals combing
options.C: Only the half filled orbitals and fully filled orbitals can participate not the empty orbital
options.D: It is not a real physical process

questions.explanation: Fully filled, half filled, empty orbitals participate in 'H' in excited state e- excited to empty orbital.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The hybridisation of N in given structure is
questions.explanation: N have three bond pair and one lone pair.. its hybridisation will be sp3

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: No. of bonding – No. of antibonding Bond order = (8 – 2)/2 = 3 Bond order of N2 = 3

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: Each oxygen atom will form 4 bonds i.e. 2 covalent bonds and 2 hydrogen bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: o-nitrophenol has intramolecular H-bonding, so it is more volatile than p-nitrophenol which has intermolecular H-bonding.

questions.explanation: H2+ has 1 e- (paramagnetic), O2- has 17 e- (paramagnetic).

questions.explanation: IBr2- and XeF2 have total number of valence electrons equal (22) and both are linear.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The species, having bond angles of 120° is
questions.explanation: BCl3 is trigonal planar with bond angle 120°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following pairs of species have the same bond order?
questions.explanation: CN- and CO both have 14 electrons and bond order 3 each.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following compounds shows the presence of intramolecular hydrogen bond?
questions.explanation: This is a direct fact from NCERT syllabus.

questions.explanation: NO2+ is linear (sp), NO3- is trigonal planar (sp2), NH4+ is tetrahedral (sp3).

questions.explanation: 2MnSO4 ⎯→ Mn2O3 n-factor = 1 ∴ Molecular weight = Equivalent weight.

questions.explanation: In divalent state, i.e. M2+ Given that = 5.92 BM ⇒ Number of unpaired electrons = 5 ⇒ 3d5 configuration ⇒ Among the given options it should be 25.

questions.explanation: In black and white photography hypo solution is used.

questions.explanation: Au + Aqua regia ⎯→ H[AuCl4].

options.B: Ce4+ and Yb2+ acquire f0 configuration

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Transuranic elements begin with
questions.explanation: Transuranic elements are the elements, having atomic number greater than 92. Atomic number of Np = 93 ⇒ Transuranic elements begin with Np.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of significant figures for the three numbers 161 cm, 0.161 cm, 0.0161 cm are
questions.explanation: All non-zero digits are significant. Non-zero digits to the right of the decimal point are significant. Zeroes to the left of the first non-zero digit in a number are not significant. So, the number of significant figures for the numbers 161 cm, 0.161 cm and 0.0161 cm are same, i.e. 3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The energies E₁ and E₂ of two radiations are 25 eV and 50 eV respectively. The relation between their wavelengths, i.e. λ₁ and λ₂ will be
questions.explanation: E₁ = 25 eV, E₂ = 50 eV. E₁ = hc/λ₁ and E₂ = hc/λ₂. Therefore E₁/E₂ = λ₂/λ₁ ⇒ 25/50 = λ₂/λ₁ ⇒ λ₁ = 2λ₂

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The value of Planck's constant is 6.63 × 10⁻³⁴ Js. The speed of light is 3 × 10¹⁷ nm s⁻¹. Which value is closest to the wavelength in nanometer of a quantum of light with frequency of 6 × 10¹⁵ s⁻¹?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The element with the atomic number 118, will be
questions.explanation: The outermost electronic configuration of element with atomic number 118 is 7s²7p⁶, so it will be a noble gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following represents the correct order of increasing electron gain enthalpy with negative sign for the elements O, S, F and Cl?
questions.explanation: Electron gain enthalpy generally increases in a period from left to right and decreases in a group on moving downward. The correct order is O < S < F < Cl.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules is non-polar in nature?
questions.explanation: SbCl₅ has trigonal bipyramidal geometry with dipole moment μ = 0, hence it is non-polar. POCl₃, CH₂O and NO₂ are polar molecules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following pairs of species have the same bond order?
questions.explanation: Species that have the same number of electrons have the same bond order. CN⁻ (6+7+1=14 electrons) and CO (6+8=14 electrons) have equal number of electrons, so their bond order will be same.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Given van der Waals' constant of NH₃, H₂, O₂ and CO₂ are respectively 4.17, 0.244, 1.36 and 3.59, which one of the following gases is most easily liquefied?
questions.explanation: In van der Waals' equation, 'a' is the measure of force of attraction between gas molecules. Greater the value of 'a', easier the liquefaction of the gas. NH₃ has the highest 'a' value (4.17), hence it is most easily liquefied.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one among the following is the correct option for right relationship between C_p and C_V for one mole of ideal gas?
questions.explanation: For an ideal gas, C_p - C_V = nR. As n = 1, so C_p - C_V = R.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Under isothermal condition, a gas at 300 K expands from 0.1 L to 0.25 L against a constant external pressure of 2 bar. The work done by the gas is (Given that 1 L bar = 100 J)
questions.explanation: For isothermal irreversible expansion, W = -p_ext(V₂ - V₁) = -2 bar × (0.25 - 0.1) L = -2 × 0.15 L bar = -0.3 L bar = -0.3 × 100 J = -30 J

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An ideal gas expands isothermally from 10⁻³ m³ to 10⁻² m³ at 300 K against a constant pressure of 10⁵ Nm⁻². The work done on the gas is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Standard enthalpy of vaporisation Δ_vapH° for water at 100°C is 40.66 kJ mol⁻¹. The internal energy of vaporisation of water at 100°C (in kJ mol⁻¹) is (assume water vapour to behave like an ideal gas)
questions.explanation: Δ_vapH° = Δ_vapE° + Δn_g RT. For H₂O(l) → H₂O(g), Δn_g = 1. Δ_vapE° = 40.66 - (8.314 × 10⁻³ × 373) = 40.66 - 3.10 = 37.56 kJ mol⁻¹

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about pH and H⁺ ion concentration is incorrect?

options.A: Addition of one drop of concentrated HCl in NH₄OH solution decreases pH of the solution
options.B: A solution of the mixture of one equivalent of each of CH₃COOH and NaOH has a pH of 7
options.C: pH of pure neutral water is not zero
options.D: A cold and concentrated H₂SO₄ has lower H⁺ ion concentration than a dilute solution of H₂SO₄

questions.explanation: CH₃COOH is weak acid while NaOH is strong base, so one equivalent of NaOH cannot be neutralized with one equivalent of CH₃COOH. Hence, one equivalent of each does not have pH value 7. The solution will be basic having pH more than 7.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The solubility of AgCl(s) with solubility product 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ in 0.1 M NaCl solution would be
questions.explanation: K_sp = [Ag⁺][Cl⁻] = s(0.1 + s). Since s is very small compared to 0.1, s² can be neglected. 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ = 0.1s ⇒ s = 1.6 × 10⁻⁹ M

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation state of Cr in CrO₅ is
questions.explanation: The structure of CrO₅ has two peroxide linkages. Let oxidation state of Cr be x. x + (-1)×4 + (-2)×1 = 0 ⇒ x - 4 - 2 = 0 ⇒ x = +6

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A compound is formed by cation C and anion A. The anions form hexagonal close packed (hcp) lattice and the cations occupy 75% of octahedral voids. The formula of the compound is
questions.explanation: Anions (A) form hcp lattice, so number of anions = 6. Number of octahedral voids = number of atoms = 6. Cations occupy 75% of octahedral voids = 6 × 75/100 = 9/2. Formula = C_{9/2}A₆ = C₉A₁₂ = C₃A₆

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Concentrated aqueous sulphuric acid is 98% H₂SO₄ by mass and has a density of 1.80 g mL⁻¹. Volume of acid required to make one litre of 0.1 M H₂SO₄ solution is
questions.explanation: Normality = (Weight percentage × density × 10)/Equivalent weight = (98 × 1.8 × 10)/49 = 36 N. N₁V₁ = N₂V₂ ⇒ 36 × V = 0.2 × 1000 ⇒ V = 5.55 mL

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molar conductivity of a 0.5 mol/dm³ solution of AgNO₃ with electrolytic conductivity of 5.76 × 10⁻³ S cm⁻¹ at 298 K is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: During the electrolysis of molten sodium chloride, the time required to produce 0.10 mol of chlorine gas using a current of 3 amperes is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction, N₂O₅(g) → 2NO₂(g) + ½O₂(g), the value of rate of disappearance of N₂O₅ is given as 6.25 × 10⁻³ mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹. The rate of formation of NO₂ and O₂ is given respectively as
questions.explanation: d[NO₂]/dt = -2 d[N₂O₅]/dt = 2 × 6.25 × 10⁻³ = 1.25 × 10⁻² mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹. d[O₂]/dt = -½ d[N₂O₅]/dt = ½ × 6.25 × 10⁻³ = 3.125 × 10⁻³ mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of the stoichiometries of AgCl formed when AgNO₃ in excess is treated with the complexes: CoCl₃·6NH₃, CoCl₃·5NH₃, CoCl₃·4NH₃ respectively is
questions.explanation: [Co(NH₃)₆]³⁺3Cl⁻ gives 3AgCl, [Co(NH₃)₅Cl]²⁺2Cl⁻ gives 2AgCl, [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]⁺Cl⁻ gives 1AgCl.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the most acidic?
questions.explanation: Acidic nature of oxides decreases down a group. So, N₂O₅ is most acidic because electronegativity of N is maximum in the given Vth group elements.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: White phosphorus on reaction with NaOH gives PH₃ as one of the products. This is a
questions.explanation: In this reaction, phosphorus undergoes both oxidation and reduction, hence it is a disproportionation reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The calculated spin only magnetic moment of Cr²⁺ ion is
questions.explanation: Electronic configuration of Cr²⁺ is [Ar]3d⁴. It has 4 unpaired electrons. Magnetic moment μ = √[n(n+2)] BM = √[4(4+2)] = √24 = 4.90 BM

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the incorrect statement.

options.A: The transition metals and their compounds are known for their catalytic activity due to their ability to adopt multiple oxidation states and to form complexes.
options.B: Interstitial compounds are those that are formed when small atoms like H, C or N are trapped inside the crystal lattices of metals.
options.C: The oxidation states of chromium in CrO₄²⁻ and Cr₂O₇²⁻ are not the same.
options.D: Cr²⁺(d⁴) is a stronger reducing agent than Fe²⁺(d⁶) in water.

questions.explanation: Oxidation states of Cr in CrO₄²⁻ and Cr₂O₇²⁻ are same (+6). CrO₄²⁻: x + 4(-2) = -2 ⇒ x = +6; Cr₂O₇²⁻: 2y + 7(-2) = -2 ⇒ y = +6.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Ethylenediaminetetraacetate (EDTA) ion is

options.A: hexadentate ligand with four O and two N donor atoms
options.C: bidentate ligand with two N donor atoms
options.D: tridentate ligand with three N donor atoms

questions.explanation: EDTA is a hexadentate ligand with four O and two N donor atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The geometry and magnetic behaviour of the complex [Ni(CO)₄] are
questions.explanation: Oxidation state of Ni in [Ni(CO)₄] is 0. CO is a strong field ligand, it pairs up the unpaired electrons. There is no unpaired electron, hence diamagnetic with tetrahedral geometry.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct increasing order of trans-effect of the following species is
questions.explanation: Trans effect order: CN⁻ < C₆H₅⁻ < Br⁻ < NH₃. As the rate of substitution of the trans ligand increases, the intensity of trans effect also increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: CH₃CH₂COO⁻Na⁺ →(NaOH + ?) Heat → CH₃CH₃ + Na₂CO₃. Consider the above reaction and identify the missing reagent/chemical.
questions.explanation: This is a decarboxylation reaction. Decarboxylation reaction takes place with soda lime (NaOH + CaO). So missing reagent is CaO.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The major product formed in dehydrohalogenation reaction of 2-bromo pentane is pent-2-ene. This product formation is based on
questions.explanation: Saytzeff's rule states that more substituted alkene is formed in a dehydrohalogenation reaction. Pent-2-ene is more substituted than pent-1-ene.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following substituted phenols is the strongest acid?
questions.explanation: Electron withdrawing groups (-NO₂) increase acidity of phenol. p-Nitrophenol has maximum -NO₂ effect at para position, making it the strongest acid among the given options.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques
questions.question: What is the IUPAC name of the organic compound formed in the following chemical reaction? Acetone →(i) C₂H₅MgBr, Dry ether →(ii) H₂O, H⁺ → product
questions.explanation: Acetone on reaction with Grignard reagent and on further hydrolysis gives 2-methylbutan-2-ol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reaction of propanamide with ethanolic sodium hydroxide and bromine will give
questions.explanation: This is Hofmann bromamide reaction through which an acid amide degrades into a one carbon less primary amine. Propanamide (C₃) gives ethylamine (C₂).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An organic compound contains 78% (by wt.) carbon and remaining percentage of hydrogen. The right option for the empirical formula of this compound is [At. wt. of C is 12, H is 1]
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The energy of second Bohr orbit of the hydrogen atom is -328 kJ mol⁻¹, hence the energy of fourth Bohr orbit would be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of decreasing second ionisation enthalpy of Ti(22), V(23), Cr(24) and Mn(25) is
questions.explanation: Second ionisation enthalpy generally increases with increase in atomic number but second ionisation potential of Cr is greater than that of Mn due to the presence of exactly half-filled d-subshell in Cr⁺. Thus, the order is Cr > Mn > V > Ti.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The species Ar, K⁺ and Ca²⁺ contain the same number of electrons. In which order do their radii increase?
questions.explanation: Ar, K⁺ and Ca²⁺ are isoelectronic (18 electrons). For isoelectronic species, ionic radii decreases with increase in effective nuclear charge. Z_eff: Ca²⁺ (20) > K⁺ (19) > Ar (18). Therefore radii: Ca²⁺ < K⁺ < Ar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The electronegativity difference between N and F is greater than that between N and H yet the dipole moment of NH₃ (1.5 D) is larger than that of NF₃ (0.2 D). This is because

options.A: in NH₃ as well as in NF₃ the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in the same direction
options.B: in NH₃ the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in the same direction whereas in NF₃ these are in opposite directions
options.C: in NH₃ as well as NF₃ the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in opposite directions
options.D: in NH₃ the atomic dipole and bond dipole are in the opposite directions whereas in NF₃ these are in the same directions

questions.explanation: In NH₃, the resultant moment of N-H bonds adds up to the bond moment of lone pair. In NF₃, the direction of net bond dipole of three N-F bonds is opposite to that of the dipole of the lone pair, causing partial cancellation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of increasing bond length of C—H, C—O, C=C and C—C is
questions.explanation: Bond lengths: C—H: 0.109 nm, C=C: 0.134 nm, C—O: 0.143 nm, C—C: 0.154 nm. Order: C—H < C=C < C—O < C—C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: At 25°C and 730 mm pressure, 380 mL of dry oxygen was collected. If the temperature is constant, what volume will the oxygen occupy at 760 mm pressure?
questions.explanation: According to Boyle's law, p₁V₁ = p₂V₂. 730 × 380 = 760 × V₂ ⇒ V₂ = (730 × 380)/760 = 365 mL.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In a closed insulated container a liquid is stirred with a paddle to increase the temperature, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: In closed insulated container, it behaves as adiabatic process, so q = 0. From first law, ΔE = q + W = 0 + W = W ≠ 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The enthalpy of combustion of H₂, cyclohexene C₆H₁₀ and cyclohexane C₆H₁₂ are -241, -3800 and -3920 kJ per mol respectively. Heat of hydrogenation of cyclohexene is
questions.explanation: C₆H₁₀ + H₂ → C₆H₁₂. ΔH = [ΔH_comb(cyclohexane)] - [ΔH_comb(cyclohexene) + ΔH_comb(H₂)] = [-3920] - [-3800 + (-241)] = -3920 + 4041 = -121 kJ/mol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
questions.question: If ΔE is the heat of reaction for C₂H₅OH(l) + 3O₂(g) → 2CO₂(g) + 3H₂O(l) at constant volume, the ΔH (heat of reaction at constant pressure), then the correct relation is
questions.explanation: ΔH = ΔE + Δn_g RT. Δn_g = moles of gaseous products - moles of gaseous reactants = 2 - 3 = -1. Therefore ΔH = ΔE - RT.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct relationship between free energy and equilibrium constant K of a reaction is
questions.explanation: The Gibbs free energy of a reaction is related to the equilibrium constant by the equation ΔG° = -RT ln K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following equilibrium K_c and K_p are not equal?
questions.explanation: K_p = K_c (RT)^Δn_g. For reaction (d), Δn_g = 2 - 1 = 1, so K_p ≠ K_c. For others, Δn_g = 0, so K_p = K_c.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: pH of a saturated solution of Ca(OH)₂ is 9. The solubility product (K_sp) of Ca(OH)₂ is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A buffer solution is prepared in which the concentration of NH₃ is 0.30 M and the concentration of NH₄⁺ is 0.20 M. If the equilibrium constant, K_b for NH₃ equals 1.8 × 10⁻⁵, what is the pH of this solution? (log 2.7 = 0.43)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Oxidation state of Fe in Fe₃O₄ is
questions.explanation: Let oxidation state of Fe be x. 3x + 4(-2) = 0 ⇒ 3x - 8 = 0 ⇒ x = 8/3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds will exhibit chirality?
questions.explanation: 3-methyl hexane contains a chiral carbon atom (carbon with four different substituents), hence it exhibits chirality.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Lithium metal crystallises in a body centred cubic (bcc) crystal. If the length of the side of the unit cell of lithium is 351 pm, the atomic radius of the lithium will be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A solution contains non-volatile solute of molecular mass M₂. Which of the following can be used to calculate the molecular mass of solute in terms of osmotic pressure?
questions.explanation: πV = nRT = (m₂/M₂)RT ⇒ M₂ = (m₂/πV)RT.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A 0.0020 m aqueous solution of an ionic compound [Co(NH₃)₅(NO₂)]Cl freezes at -0.00732°C. Number of moles of ions which 1 mole of ionic compound produces on being dissolved in water will be (k_f = -1.86°C/m)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of electrons delivered at the cathode during electrolysis by a current of 1 ampere in 60 seconds is (charge on electron = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C)
questions.explanation: Q = i × t = 1 × 60 = 60 C. Number of electrons = Q/e = 60/(1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹) = 3.75 × 10²⁰.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potential (E°) values of Al³⁺/Al, Ag⁺/Ag, K⁺/K and Cr³⁺/Cr are -1.66 V, 0.80 V, -2.93 V and -0.74 V, respectively. The correct decreasing order of reducing power of the metal is
questions.explanation: More negative the value of standard reduction potential, higher is the reducing power. Order: K (-2.93) > Al (-1.66) > Cr (-0.74) > Ag (0.80).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A first order reaction has a specific reaction rate of 10⁻² s⁻¹. How much time will it take for 20 g of the reactant to reduce to 5 g?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct for the spontaneous adsorption of a gas?

options.A: ΔS is negative and therefore, ΔH should be highly positive
options.B: ΔS is negative and therefore, ΔH should be highly negative
options.C: ΔS is positive and therefore, ΔH should be negative
options.D: ΔS is positive and therefore, ΔH should also be highly positive

questions.explanation: For adsorption of a gas, ΔS is negative because randomness decreases. For ΔG to be negative (spontaneous), ΔH must be highly negative (exothermic). ΔG = ΔH - TΔS.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following lanthanoid ions is diamagnetic? (At. no. Ce = 58, Sm = 62, Eu = 63, Yb = 70)
questions.explanation: Yb²⁺ has electronic configuration [Xe]4f¹⁴ with no unpaired electrons, hence diamagnetic. Others have unpaired electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The Crystal Field Stabilisation Energy (CFSE) for [CoCl₆]⁴⁻ is 18000 cm⁻¹. The CFSE for [CoCl₄]²⁻ will be
questions.explanation: Δ_tetrahedral = (4/9)Δ_octahedral = (4/9) × 18000 = 8000 cm⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reactivity order of halides for dehydrohalogenation is
questions.explanation: Bond dissociation energy decreases as R-F > R-Cl > R-Br > R-I. During dehydrohalogenation, R-I bond breaks most easily. Hence order: R-I > R-Br > R-Cl > R-F.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: 2-bromopentane is heated with potassium ethoxide in ethanol. The major product obtained is
questions.explanation: Dehydrohalogenation with alcoholic KOH gives more substituted alkene (Saytzeff product) with trans configuration being more stable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The compound A on treatment with Na gives B, and with PCl₅ gives C. B and C react together to give diethyl ether. A, B and C are in the order
questions.explanation: A is C₂H₅OH (ethanol). With Na gives C₂H₅ONa (B). With PCl₅ gives C₂H₅Cl (C). B and C react to give diethyl ether (Williamson's synthesis).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of the basic strength of methyl substituted amines in aqueous solution is
questions.explanation: In aqueous solution, basic strength depends on inductive effect and solvation. Order: (CH₃)₂NH > CH₃NH₂ > (CH₃)₃N.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Acetamide is treated with the following reagents separately. Which one of these would yield methyl amine?
questions.explanation: Hofmann bromamide reaction: CH₃CONH₂ + Br₂ + 4NaOH → CH₃NH₂ + Na₂CO₃ + 2NaBr + 2H₂O.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Suppose the elements X and Y combine to form two compounds XY₂ and X₃Y₂. When 0.1 mole of XY₂ weighs 10 g and 0.05 mole of X₃Y₂ weighs 9 g, the atomic weights of X and Y are
questions.explanation: For XY₂: 0.1 = 10/(A_X + 2A_Y) ⇒ A_X + 2A_Y = 100 ...(i) For X₃Y₂: 0.05 = 9/(3A_X + 2A_Y) ⇒ 3A_X + 2A_Y = 180 ...(ii) Solving (i) and (ii): A_X = 40 g mol⁻¹, A_Y = 30 g mol⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The frequency of radiation emitted when the electron falls from n = 4 to n = 1 in a hydrogen atom will be (Given ionisation energy of H = 2.18 × 10⁻¹⁸ J atom⁻¹ and h = 6.625 × 10⁻³⁴ Js)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following orders of ionic radii is correctly represented?
questions.explanation: For isoelectronic species, ionic radii ∝ 1/atomic number. Correct order: O²⁻ > F⁻ > Na⁺ > Mg²⁺ > Al³⁺.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of the decreasing ionic radii among the following isoelectronic species is
questions.explanation: For isoelectronic species, ionic radii increases with decrease in effective nuclear charge. S²⁻ (16) > Cl⁻ (17) > K⁺ (19) > Ca²⁺ (20).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Match the compounds given in column I with the hybridisation and shape given in column II.
questions.explanation: XeF₆ - Distorted octahedral, XeO₃ - Pyramidal, XeOF₄ - Square pyramidal, XeF₄ - Square planar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: XeF₂ is isostructural with
questions.explanation: Species having the same number of bond pairs and lone pairs are isostructural. XeF₂ has 4 lp + 2 bp (linear). ICl₂⁻ also has 4 lp + 2 bp (linear).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: 600 cc of a gas at a pressure of 750 mm is compressed to 500 cc. Taking the temperature to remain constant, the increase in pressure is
questions.explanation: p₁V₁ = p₂V₂ ⇒ 750 × 600 = p₂ × 500 ⇒ p₂ = (750 × 600)/500 = 900 mm. Increase in pressure = 900 - 750 = 150 mm.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the gas phase reaction, PCl₅(g) → PCl₃(g) + Cl₂(g) which of the following conditions are correct?
questions.explanation: Δn_g = 2 - 1 = 1, so ΔS > 0. The reaction is endothermic (bond breaking), so ΔH > 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: What is the entropy change (in J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹) when one mole of ice is converted into water at 0°C? (The enthalpy change for the conversion of ice to liquid water is 6.0 kJ mol⁻¹ at 0°C)
questions.explanation: ΔS = ΔH_f/T = 6000 J mol⁻¹/273 K = 21.98 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Unit of entropy is
questions.explanation: Entropy change ΔS = q/T, so unit is JK⁻¹ mol⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding entropy.

options.A: At absolute zero temperature, entropy of a perfectly crystalline substance is taken to be zero
options.B: At absolute zero temperature, the entropy of a perfectly crystalline substance is positive
options.C: At absolute zero temperature, the entropy of all crystalline substances is to be zero
options.D: At 0°C the entropy of a perfectly crystalline substance is taken to be zero

questions.explanation: According to third law of thermodynamics, at absolute zero temperature, entropy of a perfectly crystalline substance is taken to be zero.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which will make basic buffer?

options.A: 100 mL of 0.1 M CH₃COOH + 100 mL of 0.1 M NaOH
options.B: 100 mL of 0.1 M HCl + 200 mL of 0.1 M NH₄OH
options.C: 100 mL of 0.1 M HCl + 100 mL of 0.1 M NaOH
options.D: 50 mL of 0.1 M NaOH + 25 mL of 0.1 M CH₃COOH

questions.explanation: Basic buffer is obtained by mixing weak base and its salt with strong acid. In option (b), after reaction, solution contains NH₄OH (weak base) and NH₄Cl (salt).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In acidic medium, H₂O₂ changes Cr₂O₇²⁻ to CrO₅ which has two (—O—O—) bonds. Oxidation state of Cr in CrO₅ is
questions.explanation: CrO₅ has two peroxide linkages. Let oxidation state of Cr be x. x + (-1)×4 + (-2)×1 = 0 ⇒ x - 4 - 2 = 0 ⇒ x = +6.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Lithium has a bcc structure. Its density is 530 kg m⁻³ and its atomic mass is 6.94 g mol⁻¹. Calculate the edge length of a unit cell of lithium metal. (N_A = 6.02 × 10²³ mol⁻¹)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The mole fraction of the solute in one molar aqueous solution is
questions.explanation: 1 m solution = 1 mole solute in 1000 g solvent. Moles of solvent = 1000/18 = 55.55. Mole fraction of solute = 1/(1 + 55.55) = 1/56.55 = 0.0177 ≈ 0.018.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: pA and pB are the vapour pressure of pure liquid components A and B, respectively of an ideal binary solution. If χA represents the mole fraction of component A, the total pressure of the solution will be
questions.explanation: p_T = p_A χA + p_B χB = p_A χA + p_B (1 - χA) = p_B + (p_A - p_B)χA.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the cell reaction, 2Fe³⁺(aq) + 2I⁻(aq) → 2Fe²⁺(aq) + I₂(aq), E_cell° = 0.24 V at 298 K. The standard Gibbs energy (ΔrG°) of the cell reaction is [Given that Faraday constant F = 96500 C mol⁻¹]

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The equilibrium constant of the reaction, Cu(s) + 2Ag⁺(aq) → Cu²⁺(aq) + 2Ag(s), E° = 0.46 V at 298 K is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The half-life for a zero order reaction having 0.02 M initial concentration of reactant is 100 s. The rate constant (in mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹) for the reaction is
questions.explanation: For zero order reaction, t₁/₂ = [A]₀/(2k) ⇒ k = [A]₀/(2t₁/₂) = 0.02/(2 × 100) = 1.0 × 10⁻⁴ mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A first order reaction has a rate constant 0.2303 × 10⁻³ s⁻¹. The time required for 40 g of this reactant to reduce to 10 g will be [Given that log₁₀ 2 = 0.3010]
questions.explanation: t = (2.303/k) log(a/(a-x)) = (2.303/0.2303 × 10⁻³) log(40/10) = 10⁴ × log 4 = 10⁴ × 0.602 = 6020 s? Wait calculation: 2.303/0.2303 × 10⁻³ = 10⁴? Actually 2.303/0.2303 = 10, then × 10⁻³ gives 10⁴? Let's recalc properly: k = 0.2303 × 10⁻³ = 2.303 × 10⁻⁴. 2.303/k = 2.303/(2.303 × 10⁻⁴) = 10⁴. t = 10⁴ × log(40/10) = 10⁴ × log 4 = 10⁴ × 0.602 = 6020 s. None of the options match. Actually the options might be in different units or the given k value might be different.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If x is amount of adsorbate and m is amount of adsorbent, which of the following relations is not related to adsorption process?
questions.explanation: x/m = p × T is incorrect. The correct relation is x/m ∝ p/T (for adsorption).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following fluoro-compounds is most likely to behave as a Lewis base?
questions.explanation: PF₃ has a lone pair on P atom, so it can act as a Lewis base (electron pair donor). Others do not have lone pairs on central atom.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of N-compounds in its decreasing order of oxidation states is
questions.explanation: Oxidation states: HNO₃ (+5), NO (+2), N₂ (0), NH₄Cl (-3). Decreasing order: HNO₃ > NO > N₂ > NH₄Cl.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When neutral or faintly alkaline KMnO₄ is treated with potassium iodide, iodide ion is converted into 'X'. 'X' is
questions.explanation: 2MnO₄⁻ + I⁻ + H₂O → 2MnO₂ + IO₃⁻ + 2OH⁻. Iodide is oxidized to iodate (IO₃⁻).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Match the metal ions given in Column I with the spin magnetic moments of the ions given in Column II.
questions.explanation: μ = √[n(n+2)] BM. Co³⁺ (d⁶, low spin, n=0? Actually Co³⁺ can have n=4 in high spin? Wait calculation: Co³⁺ d⁶ high spin n=4, μ=√24; Cr³⁺ d³ n=3, μ=√15; Fe³⁺ d⁵ n=5, μ=√35; Ni²⁺ d⁸ n=2, μ=√8.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Iron carbonyl, Fe(CO)₅ is
questions.explanation: Fe(CO)₅ contains only one central metal atom, hence it is mononuclear.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An example of a sigma bonded organometallic compound is
questions.explanation: Grignard reagent (R-MgX) has a sigma bond between carbon and metal. Ruthenocene, ferrocene and cobaltocene have π-bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Pick out the correct statement with respect to [Mn(CN)₆]³⁻

options.A: It is sp³d² hybridised and octahedral
options.B: It is sp³d² hybridised and tetrahedral
options.C: It is d²sp³ hybridised and octahedral
options.D: It is dsp² hybridised and square planar

questions.explanation: [Mn(CN)₆]³⁻ is d²sp³ hybridised and octahedral. Mn is in +3 oxidation state with d⁴ configuration, but CN⁻ being strong field ligand causes pairing.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In a S_N2 substitution reaction of the type R-Br + Cl⁻ →(DMF) → R-Cl + Br⁻, which one of the following has the highest relative rate?
questions.explanation: S_N2 reactions are favoured by less steric hindrance. CH₃-Br is least hindered, hence has highest relative rate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will not be soluble in sodium hydrogen carbonate?
questions.explanation: o-Nitrophenol is less acidic due to intramolecular H-bonding and does not react with NaHCO₃. Others are strong acids that react with NaHCO₃.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Given are cyclohexanol (I), acetic acid (II), 2,4,6-trinitrophenol (III) and phenol (IV). In these, the order of decreasing acidic character will be
questions.explanation: 2,4,6-trinitrophenol (picric acid) is strongest due to three -NO₂ groups. Acetic acid > phenol > cyclohexanol. Order: III > II > IV > I.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Propionic acid with Br₂/P yields a dibromo product. Its structure would be
questions.explanation: HVZ reaction: Carboxylic acids with Br₂/red P give α-bromo acids. Propionic acid gives 2-bromopropionic acid, then further bromination gives 2,2-dibromopropionic acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about primary amines is false?

options.A: Alkyl amines are stronger bases than aryl amines
options.B: Alkyl amines react with nitrous acid to produce alcohols
options.C: Aryl amines react with nitrous acid to produce phenols
options.D: Alkyl amines are stronger bases than ammonia

questions.explanation: Aryl amines react with nitrous acid to form diazonium salts, not phenols. Phenols are obtained only when diazonium salts are hydrolyzed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of atoms in 0.1 mole of a triatomic gas is (N_A = 6.023 × 10²³ mol⁻¹)
questions.explanation: Number of atoms = number of moles × N_A × atomicity = 0.1 × 6.023 × 10²³ × 3 = 1.806 × 10²³ atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In hydrogen atom, energy of first excited state is -3.4 eV. Then, KE of same orbit of hydrogen atom is
questions.explanation: Total energy E = -3.4 eV. In Bohr's model, KE = -E = +3.4 eV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Among the following which one is the wrong statement?

options.A: Amongst isoelectronic species, smaller the positive charge on the cation, smaller is the ionic radius
options.B: Amongst isoelectronic species, greater the negative charge on the anion, larger is the ionic radius
options.C: Atomic radius of the elements increases as one moves down the first group of the periodic table
options.D: Atomic radius of the elements decreases as one moves across from left to right in the 2nd period of the periodic table

questions.explanation: Amongst isoelectronic species, ionic radius increases with decrease in positive charge or increase in negative charge. So statement (a) is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the most basic oxide?
questions.explanation: As we move down the group, basic nature of oxides increases. Bi₂O₃ is most basic among the given oxides.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following diatomic molecular species has only π-bonds according to molecular orbital theory?
questions.explanation: C₂ has configuration: σ₁ₛ² σ₁ₛ² σ₂ₛ² σ₂ₛ² π₂ₚₓ² = π₂ₚᵧ². The double bond consists of both π-bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of antibonding electron pairs in O₂²⁻ molecular ion on the basis of molecular orbital theory is (at. no. of O is 8)
questions.explanation: O₂²⁻ has 18 electrons. Configuration: σ₁ₛ² σ₁ₛ² σ₂ₛ² σ₂ₛ² σ₂ₚz² π₂ₚₓ² π₂ₚᵧ² π₂ₚₓ² π₂ₚᵧ². Antibonding electron pairs = 4 (σ₁ₛ, σ₂ₛ, π₂ₚₓ, π₂ₚᵧ).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The ratio among most probable velocity, mean velocity and root mean square velocity is given by
questions.explanation: v_mp = √(2RT/M), v_av = √(8RT/πM), v_rms = √(3RT/M). Ratio = √2 : √(8/π) : √3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molar heat capacity C of water at constant pressure is 75 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹, when 1.0 kJ of heat is supplied to 100 g of water which is free to expand, the increase in temperature of water is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In an endothermic reaction, the value of ΔH is
questions.explanation: For endothermic reactions, heat is absorbed, so ΔH is positive.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: One mole of an ideal gas at 300 K is expanded isothermally from an initial volume of 1 L to 10 L. The ΔE for this process is (R = 2 cal mol⁻¹ K⁻¹)
questions.explanation: For isothermal process, temperature remains constant, so internal energy (ΔE) also remains constant. ΔE = 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: During isothermal expansion of an ideal gas, its
questions.explanation: H = E + PV. For isothermal expansion of ideal gas, ΔT = 0, so ΔE = 0 and Δ(PV) = 0, hence ΔH = 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The dissociation constants for acetic acid and HCN at 25°C are 1.5 × 10⁻⁵ and 4.5 × 10⁻¹⁰ respectively. The equilibrium constant for the equilibrium, CN⁻ + CH₃COOH ⇌ HCN + CH₃COO⁻ would be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the concentration of OH⁻ ions in the reaction, Fe(OH)₃(s) ⇌ Fe³⁺(aq) + 3OH⁻(aq) is decreased by 1/4 times, then equilibrium concentration of Fe³⁺ will increase by
questions.explanation: K = [Fe³⁺][OH⁻]³. To maintain K constant, when [OH⁻] becomes 1/4, [Fe³⁺] must become 64 times. [Fe³⁺]' × (1/4)³ = [Fe³⁺] ⇒ [Fe³⁺]' = 64 [Fe³⁺].

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The value of ΔH for the reaction, X₂(g) + 4Y₂(g) ⇌ 2XY₄(g) is less than zero. Formation of XY₄(g) will be favoured at
questions.explanation: ΔH < 0 (exothermic) so low temperature favours forward reaction. Δn_g = 2 - 5 = -3 (decrease in moles), so high pressure favours forward reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation state of Cr in K₂Cr₂O₇ is
questions.explanation: Let oxidation state of Cr be x. 2(+1) + 2x + 7(-2) = 0 ⇒ 2 + 2x - 14 = 0 ⇒ 2x = 12 ⇒ x = +6.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Copper crystallises in a face centred cubic (fcc) lattice with a unit cell length of 361 pm. What is the radius of copper atom in pm?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Of the following 0.10 m aqueous solutions, which one will exhibit the largest freezing point depression?
questions.explanation: ΔT_f = i × k_f × m. i for Al₂(SO₄)₃ = 5, highest among given. KCl (i=2), K₂SO₄ (i=3), glucose (i=1).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The van't Hoff factor (i) for a compound which undergoes dissociation in one solvent and association in other solvent is respectively

options.A: less than one and less than one
options.B: greater than one and less than one
options.C: greater than one and greater than one
options.D: less than one and greater than one

questions.explanation: For dissociation, i > 1; for association, i < 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Standard electrode potentials are Fe²⁺/Fe (E° = -0.44 V), Fe³⁺/Fe²⁺ (E° = 0.77 V). Fe²⁺, Fe³⁺ and Fe block are kept together, then
questions.explanation: Fe can reduce Fe³⁺ to Fe²⁺. So Fe³⁺ decreases and Fe²⁺ increases. Option (b) says Fe²⁺ decreases which is false.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Cu⁺(aq) is unstable in solution and undergoes simultaneous oxidation and reduction according to the reaction 2Cu⁺(aq) ⇌ Cu²⁺(aq) + Cu(s). Choose the correct E° for above reaction if E°(Cu²⁺/Cu) = 0.34 V and E°(Cu²⁺/Cu⁺) = 0.15 V

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In a zero order reaction for every 10°C rise of temperature, the rate is doubled. If the temperature is increased from 10°C to 100°C, the rate of the reaction will become
questions.explanation: Number of 10°C intervals = (100 - 10)/10 = 9. Rate becomes 2⁹ = 512 times.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For an endothermic reaction, energy of activation is E_a and enthalpy of reaction is ΔH (both in kJ/mol). Minimum value of E_a will be
questions.explanation: For endothermic reaction, E_a(forward) = E_a(backward) + ΔH. So E_a > ΔH.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For adsorption of a gas on a solid, the plot of log (x/m) vs log p is linear with slope equal to (n being a whole number)
questions.explanation: According to Freundlich adsorption isotherm, x/m = k p¹/ⁿ. Taking log: log(x/m) = log k + (1/n) log p. Slope = 1/n.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which pair of ions both the species contain S—S bond?
questions.explanation: S₄O₆²⁻ (tetrathionate) and S₂O₃²⁻ (thiosulphate) both contain S—S bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Hot concentrated sulphuric acid is a moderately strong oxidising agent. Which of the following reaction does not show oxidising behaviour?
questions.explanation: In reaction (d), oxidation numbers of elements remain unchanged. H₂SO₄ does not act as oxidising agent; it acts as acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of thermal stability for H₂E (E = O, S, Se, Te and Po) is
questions.explanation: Thermal stability decreases down the group due to increase in bond length and decrease in bond enthalpy. Order: H₂Po < H₂Te < H₂Se < H₂S < H₂O.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following pairs are both the ions coloured in aqueous solution? (At. no. Sc = 21, Ti = 22, Ni = 28, Cu = 29, Co = 27)
questions.explanation: Ni²⁺ (d⁸) and Ti³⁺ (d¹) have unpaired electrons, hence coloured. Sc³⁺ (d⁰) and Cu⁺ (d¹⁰) are colourless.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Crystal field stabilisation energy for high spin d⁴ octahedral complex is
questions.explanation: For high spin d⁴, configuration is t₂g³ eg¹. CFSE = (-0.4 × 3) + (0.6 × 1) = -1.2 + 0.6 = -0.6 Δ₀.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Out of TiF₆²⁻, CoF₆³⁻, Cu₂Cl₂ and NiCl₄²⁻ (At. no. Z of Ti = 22, Co = 27, Cu = 29, Ni = 28), the colourless species are
questions.explanation: TiF₆²⁻ has Ti⁴⁺ (d⁰) and Cu₂Cl₂ has Cu⁺ (d¹⁰), both have no unpaired electrons, hence colourless.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When CH₃CH₂CHCl₂ is treated with NaNH₂, the product formed is
questions.explanation: 1,1-dichloropropane with NaNH₂ undergoes dehydrohalogenation twice to form propyne (CH₃—CH₂—C≡CH).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The compound which shows metamerism is
questions.explanation: Metamerism is shown by compounds having same molecular formula but different number of carbon atoms on either side of functional group. C₄H₁₀O (ether) shows metamerism: CH₃-O-C₃H₇ and C₂H₅-O-C₂H₅.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Acetone is treated with excess of ethanol in the presence of hydrochloric acid. The product obtained is
questions.explanation: Acetone with excess ethanol in presence of HCl forms ketal: (CH₃)₂C(OC₂H₅)₂.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct statement regarding the basicity of arylamines is

options.A: Arylamines are generally more basic than alkylamines because the nitrogen lone-pair electrons are not delocalized by interaction with the aromatic ring π-electron system
options.B: Arylamines are generally more basic than alkylamines because of aryl group
options.C: Arylamines are generally more basic than alkylamines, because the nitrogen atom in arylamines is sp-hybridized
options.D: Arylamines are generally less basic than alkylamines because the nitrogen lone-pair electrons are delocalized by interaction with the aromatic ring π-electron system

questions.explanation: In arylamines, lone pair of nitrogen is delocalized over benzene ring, reducing electron density on nitrogen, hence less basic than alkylamines.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: 6.02 × 10²⁰ molecules of urea are present in 100 mL of its solution. The concentration of solution is
questions.explanation: Number of moles = 6.02 × 10²⁰/(6.023 × 10²³) = 0.001 mol. Volume = 100 mL = 0.1 L. Molarity = 0.001/0.1 = 0.01 M.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Bohr radius for the hydrogen atom (n = 1) is approximately 0.530 Å. The radius for the first excited state (n = 2) is (in Å)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of increasing thermal stability of K₂CO₃, MgCO₃, CaCO₃ and BeCO₃ is
questions.explanation: Thermal stability of carbonates increases in a group as we move from top to bottom and decreases in a period as we move from left to right. Order: BeCO₃ < MgCO₃ < CaCO₃ < K₂CO₃.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following ions will be smallest in size?
questions.explanation: Na⁺, Mg²⁺, O²⁻ and F⁻ are isoelectronic but Mg²⁺ has the highest nuclear charge (12), so the attraction force on last shell is maximum and hence, it has smallest size.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In NO₃⁻ ion number of bond pair and lone pair of electrons on nitrogen atom are
questions.explanation: In NO₃⁻ ion, nitrogen has four bond pairs and zero lone pair of electrons, due to the presence of one coordinate bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following, bond angle is maximum?
questions.explanation: In NH₄⁺, bond angle is maximum (109°28') due to its tetrahedral geometry with no lone pair.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In PO₄³⁻ ion, the formal charge on each oxygen atom and P—O bond order respectively are
questions.explanation: Bond order = Total number of bonds/Total number of resonating structures = 5/4 = 1.25. Average formal charge on each O-atom = -3/4 = -0.75.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one is not paramagnetic among the following? [at. no. of Be = 4, Ne = 10, As = 33, Cl = 17]
questions.explanation: Cl⁻ has no unpaired electron, so it is diamagnetic. All others have unpaired electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A gas is said to behave like an ideal gas when the relation pV/T = constant. When do you expect a real gas to behave like an ideal gas?

options.B: When both the temperature and pressure are low
options.C: When both the temperature and pressure are high
options.D: When the temperature is high and pressure is low

questions.explanation: Real gases show ideal behaviour when pressure approaches zero and temperature is high. At this condition, there is no force of attraction and repulsion between molecules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If ΔH is the change in enthalpy and ΔE the change in internal energy accompanying a gaseous reaction, then

options.A: ΔH is always greater than ΔE
options.B: ΔH < ΔE only if the number of moles of products is greater than the number of moles of the reactants
options.C: ΔH is always less than ΔE
options.D: ΔH < ΔE only if the number of moles of products is less than the number of moles of the reactants

questions.explanation: ΔH = ΔE + Δn_g RT. When Δn_g is negative, ΔH < ΔE.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Standard free energies of formation (in kJ/mol) at 298 K are -237.2, -394.4 and -8.2 for H₂O(l), CO₂(g) and pentane (g), respectively. The value of E_cell° for the pentane-oxygen fuel cell is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Standard Gibbs free energy change for the isomerisation reaction cis-2-pentene ⇌ trans-2-pentene is -3.67 kJ/mol at 400 K. If more trans-2-pentene is added to the reaction vessel, then

options.D: equilibrium is shifted in forward direction

questions.explanation: According to Le-Chatelier's principle, when we increase the concentration of trans-2-pentene, the reaction shifts in backward direction and hence, the concentration of cis-2-pentene increases to maintain K constant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The pH value of blood does not change appreciably by a small addition of an acid or base, because the blood

options.C: contains iron as a part of the molecule
options.D: contains serum protein that acts as buffer

questions.explanation: Blood is an example of buffer solution, which contains serum protein, so its pH does not change appreciably by adding small amount of an acid or a base to it.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The pH value of a 10 M solution of HCl is
questions.explanation: [H⁺] = 10 M. pH = -log[H⁺] = -log 10 = -1, so pH is less than zero.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Equimolar solutions of the following were prepared in water separately. Which one of the solutions will record the highest pH?
questions.explanation: All salts are soluble in water and give strong acid and weak base. The basic nature of alkaline earth metals generally increases from Be to Ra. Thus, order of basic nature: Mg(OH)₂ < Ca(OH)₂ < Sr(OH)₂ < Ba(OH)₂. Hence, pH is highest for BaCl₂.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following compounds, transition metal has zero oxidation state?
questions.explanation: Oxidation state of Fe in Fe(CO)₅ is zero because CO is a neutral ligand.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: 0.24 g of a volatile gas, upon vaporisation, gives 45 mL vapour at NTP. What will be the vapour density of the substance? (Density of H₂ = 0.089)
questions.explanation: Weight of gas = 0.24 g. Volume of gas = 45 mL = 0.045 L. Weight of 45 mL of H₂ = V × d = 0.045 × 0.089 = 4.005 × 10⁻³. Vapour density = Weight of certain volume of substance/Weight of same volume of hydrogen = 0.24/(4.005 × 10⁻³) = 59.93.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The radius of hydrogen atom in the ground state is 5.3 × 10⁻¹¹ m. After collision with an electron it is found to have a radius of 21.2 × 10⁻¹¹ m. What is the principal quantum number n of the final state of the atom?
questions.explanation: r ∝ n². r₂/r₁ = n₂²/n₁² ⇒ 21.2 × 10⁻¹¹/(5.3 × 10⁻¹¹) = n₂²/1² ⇒ 4 = n₂² ⇒ n₂ = 2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The first ionisation potential (in eV) of Be and B, respectively are
questions.explanation: Be has stable configuration 2s², so more energy is required to remove an electron from 2s orbital in comparison to 2p orbital. So, ionisation potential of Be is 9.32 eV and B is 8.29 eV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In crystals of which of the following ionic compounds would you expect maximum distance between centres of cations and anions?
questions.explanation: On moving from top to bottom in a group, distance between ions in ionic compounds increases. Hence, it is maximum in CsI.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Root mean square velocity of a gas molecule is proportional to
questions.explanation: v_rms = √(3RT/M). Since M = mN_A, v_rms ∝ 1/√m ⇒ v_rms ∝ m⁻¹/².

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a given reaction, ΔH = 35.5 kJ mol⁻¹ and ΔS = 83.6 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹. The reaction is spontaneous at: (Assume that ΔH and ΔS do not vary with temperature)
questions.explanation: For spontaneity, ΔG = ΔH - TΔS < 0 ⇒ T > ΔH/ΔS = 35500/83.6 = 425 K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a sample of perfect gas when its pressure is changed isothermally from pᵢ to p_f, the entropy change is given by
questions.explanation: For isothermal process, ΔS = nR ln(V_f/V_i) = nR ln(pᵢ/p_f).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct for a reversible process in a state of equilibrium?
questions.explanation: At equilibrium, ΔG = 0 and ΔG° = -RT ln K = -2.303 RT log K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The van't Hoff factor, i for a compound which undergoes dissociation in one solvent and association in other solvent is respectively

options.A: less than one and less than one
options.B: greater than one and less than one
options.C: greater than one and greater than one
options.D: less than one and greater than one

questions.explanation: For dissociation, i > 1; for association, i < 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The freezing point depression constant for water is -1.86°C m⁻¹. If 5.00 g Na₂SO₄ is dissolved in 45.0 g H₂O, the freezing point is changed by -3.82°C. Calculate the van't Hoff factor for Na₂SO₄.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of covalent bonding?
questions.explanation: Covalent compounds generally have low melting points due to weak intermolecular forces. High melting points are characteristic of ionic compounds and network covalent solids.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT have a trigonal planar geometry?
questions.explanation: NH₃ has pyramidal geometry due to one lone pair. BF₃, AlCl₃ monomer, and BCl₃ have trigonal planar geometry (sp² hybridization, no lone pairs).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correct about bond polarity?

options.A: More electronegativity difference means more polarity

questions.explanation: Diatomic molecules with identical atoms (H₂, N₂, O₂, Cl₂) are non-polar. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT exhibit resonance?
questions.explanation: CO₂ has two equivalent resonance structures but they are identical, so it's often considered not showing resonance in the same way as others. Benzene, O₃, and CO₃²⁻ show significant resonance delocalization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of ionic compounds?
questions.explanation: Ionic compounds are generally insoluble in non-polar organic solvents. They dissolve in polar solvents like water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT have a linear shape?
questions.explanation: H₂O has bent shape (104.5°). CO₂ (180°), C₂H₂ (180°), and BeCl₂ (180°) are linear molecules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an intermolecular force?
questions.explanation: Covalent bond is intramolecular (within molecule). Hydrogen bonding, dipole-dipole, and London forces are intermolecular forces.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT follow Markovnikov's rule for addition of HBr?
questions.explanation: In presence of peroxide, HBr adds via anti-Markovnikov rule (free radical mechanism). Without peroxide, it follows Markovnikov rule.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an alkane?
questions.explanation: C₄H₈ corresponds to alkene (butene) or cycloalkane. Alkanes have formula CₙH₂ₙ₊₂. C₄H₁₀ is butane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds does NOT show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: 1-butene has CH₂= group with two identical H atoms on one carbon, so no geometrical isomerism. Others have different groups on each doubly bonded carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an aromatic compound?
questions.explanation: Cyclohexane is alicyclic (saturated) with no delocalized π electrons. Others are aromatic with conjugated π systems following Hückel's rule.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following trends is INCORRECT?

options.A: Atomic radius increases down a group
options.B: Atomic radius decreases across a period
options.C: Ionic radius of cation > parent atom
options.D: Ionic radius of anion > parent atom

questions.explanation: Cation is smaller than parent atom due to loss of electron and reduced shielding. Anion is larger. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest ionization energy?
questions.explanation: Be has higher IE than Li, B, and C due to stable s² configuration. Among these, Be has highest IE (actually N has higher, but not in options).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a halogen?
questions.explanation: S (sulfur) is in group 16 (chalcogens). Halogens are group 17 elements: F, Cl, Br, I, At.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about electronegativity?

options.A: It is the ability to attract shared electrons

questions.explanation: Noble gases have very low electronegativity (almost zero) as they have stable configurations and no tendency to attract electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an alkali metal?
questions.explanation: Mg is alkaline earth metal (group 2). Alkali metals are group 1 elements: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of ionic bonding?
questions.explanation: Ionic compounds have high melting points due to strong electrostatic forces. Low melting points are characteristic of covalent molecular compounds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules is NOT paramagnetic?
questions.explanation: N₂ has all electrons paired (diamagnetic). O₂, NO, and O₂⁻ have unpaired electrons and are paramagnetic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT have a bent shape?
questions.explanation: CO₂ is linear (O=C=O). H₂O, SO₂, and NO₂ have bent shapes due to lone pairs on central atom.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correct about hydrogen bonding?

options.A: It is stronger than van der Waals forces
options.B: It occurs in HF, H₂O, NH₃
options.C: It is a type of covalent bond

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is an intermolecular force, not a covalent bond. It is much weaker than covalent bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT show resonance?
questions.explanation: CH₄ has only single bonds with no delocalization of electrons. Others show resonance with delocalized π electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following reactions does NOT follow anti-Markovnikov rule?
questions.explanation: In dark (absence of light/peroxide), HBr follows Markovnikov rule. Peroxide, UV light, and benzoyl peroxide promote free radical anti-Markovnikov addition.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds does NOT undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction?
questions.explanation: Nitrobenzene has strong electron-withdrawing –NO₂ group that deactivates the ring, making it unable to undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction. Chlorobenzene undergoes reaction slowly.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an electrophile?
questions.explanation: OH⁻ has lone pairs and acts as nucleophile (electron-rich). H⁺, NO₂⁺, and Br⁺ are electron-deficient and act as electrophiles.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a nucleophile?
questions.explanation: AlCl₃ is electron-deficient and acts as Lewis acid (electrophile). NH₃, CN⁻, and H₂O have lone pairs and act as nucleophiles.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT show tautomerism?
questions.explanation: Benzaldehyde has no α-hydrogen, so no keto-enol tautomerism. Acetone has α-H, nitromethane shows nitro-aci tautomerism, propanal has α-H.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about ionization energy?

options.C: IE of noble gases is very high
options.D: IE of alkali metals is very high

questions.explanation: Alkali metals have low ionization energies because they can easily lose their single valence electron. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following pairs is NOT isoelectronic?
questions.explanation: All given pairs have 10 electrons: Na⁺ (11-1), O²⁻ (8+2), F⁻ (9+1), Mg²⁺ (12-2), Ne (10). All are isoelectronic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a transition element?
questions.explanation: Zn has electronic configuration [Ar]3d¹⁰4s² (fully filled d-orbital), so it is not considered a transition element. Others have partially filled d-orbitals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following elements does NOT belong to the same period?
questions.explanation: Na, Mg, Al are in period 3. Ca is in period 4. So Ca does not belong to the same period as others.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about electron gain enthalpy?

options.A: It can be positive or negative
options.C: Noble gases have high negative values

questions.explanation: Noble gases have positive or zero electron gain enthalpy due to stable configurations. They do not have high negative values. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about bond order?
questions.explanation: Bond order of He₂ is 0 (does not exist). MO configuration: σ1s² σ*1s², bond order = (2-2)/2 = 0. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT have a tetrahedral structure?
questions.explanation: XeF₄ has square planar geometry (sp³d², two lone pairs). CH₄, NH₄⁺, and SO₄²⁻ have tetrahedral geometry.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an electron deficient compound?
questions.explanation: C₂H₆ (ethane) has complete octet for all atoms (normal covalent). BF₃ (6 e⁻), AlCl₃ (6 e⁻), and B₂H₆ (electron deficient with 3c-2e bonds) are electron deficient.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following reactions does NOT give benzaldehyde as product?
questions.explanation: This is Friedel-Crafts acylation giving acetophenone, not benzaldehyde. Others are: (a) Etard reaction, (b) Rosenmund reduction, (c) Gattermann-Koch synthesis - all give benzaldehyde.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT undergo nucleophilic substitution easily?
questions.explanation: Vinyl chloride (CH₂=CH–Cl) has partial double bond character due to resonance between Cl lone pair and C=C, making C–Cl bond strong and resistant to nucleophilic substitution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct statement about benzene?

options.D: It is more stable than cyclohexatriene

questions.explanation: Benzene does not decolourise bromine water under normal conditions as it undergoes substitution, not addition. It requires catalyst for reaction with bromine.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds does NOT give positive iodoform test?
questions.explanation: 1-propanol does not have CH₃–CH(OH)– group (it has –CH₂OH). Iodoform test requires CH₃–CO– or CH₃–CH(OH)– group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a reducing sugar?
questions.explanation: Sucrose has glycosidic linkage between anomeric carbons of glucose and fructose, so no free aldehyde/ketone group, hence non-reducing. Others have free carbonyl group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following trends is INCORRECT for group 16 elements?

options.A: Atomic radius increases down the group
options.B: Ionization energy decreases down the group
options.C: Electron affinity increases down the group
options.D: Non-metallic character decreases down the group

questions.explanation: Electron affinity generally decreases down the group (with exceptions). For group 16, O has lower EA than S due to small size and electron repulsion. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about lanthanoids?
questions.explanation: Most lanthanoids are not radioactive (except promethium). Actinoids are radioactive. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest electronegativity?
questions.explanation: Fluorine has highest electronegativity (4.0). This is a factual statement, not an incorrect one.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an actinoid?
questions.explanation: Ce (cerium, Z=58) is a lanthanoid. Actinoids are elements from Th (90) to Lr (103). Th, U, Pu are actinoids.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about atomic radius is INCORRECT?

options.A: Atomic radius decreases across a period
options.B: Atomic radius increases down a group
options.C: van der Waals radius > covalent radius
options.D: Metallic radius > covalent radius for same atom

questions.explanation: Metallic radius is about half the distance between adjacent atoms in metal crystal. Covalent radius is half the distance in covalent bond. They are comparable, not always one greater. Statement is questionable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT show variable oxidation states?
questions.explanation: Zn shows only +2 oxidation state (d¹⁰ configuration, stable). Mn (+2 to +7), Fe (+2,+3), Cu (+1,+2) show variable oxidation states.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about ionic compounds?

options.D: They conduct electricity in solid state

questions.explanation: Ionic compounds do not conduct electricity in solid state because ions are fixed in lattice and cannot move. They conduct only in molten or aqueous state.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct statement about hydrogen bonding?

options.C: It occurs only in HF, H₂O, NH₃

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding occurs in many compounds with H bonded to F, O, or N (alcohols, carboxylic acids, amines, proteins, DNA). Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correct about bond length?

options.B: Bond length increases with atomic size
options.C: Bond length decreases with bond order
options.D: Bond length of O₂ is greater than O₂⁺

questions.explanation: O₂⁺ has bond order 2.5, O₂ has bond order 2. Higher bond order means shorter bond length, so O₂⁺ has shorter bond length. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following reactions does NOT follow Saytzeff rule?
questions.explanation: 1-bromo-2-methylpropane gives mainly 2-methylpropene (Hofmann product) due to steric hindrance, not the more substituted alkene (there's only one product). Saytzeff rule applies when multiple alkenes possible.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an electrophilic substitution reaction?
questions.explanation: Halogenation of alkane is free radical substitution, not electrophilic. Nitration, sulphonation, and halogenation of benzene are electrophilic substitution reactions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT react with ammoniacal AgNO₃?
questions.explanation: 2-butyne is an internal alkyne with no terminal H, so does not react with ammoniacal AgNO₃. Terminal alkynes (1-butyne, acetylene, propyne) give white precipitate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a method to distinguish between 1-butyne and 2-butyne?
questions.explanation: Both have same molecular formula (C₄H₆), so combustion analysis gives same results. Tollen's test, NaNH₂, and ammoniacal AgNO₃ react only with terminal alkynes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about electronegativity is INCORRECT?

options.D: Electronegativity of noble gases is very high

questions.explanation: Noble gases have very low electronegativity (almost zero) due to stable electron configurations. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following elements does NOT belong to the same group?
questions.explanation: F, Cl, Br are halogens (group 17). O belongs to group 16 (chalcogens). So O does not belong to the same group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following trends is INCORRECT for group 1 elements?

options.A: Atomic radius increases down the group
options.B: Ionization energy decreases down the group
options.D: Melting point increases down the group

questions.explanation: Melting point decreases down the group for alkali metals (Na 97.7°C, K 63.5°C, Rb 39.3°C, Cs 28.4°C). Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of noble gases?
questions.explanation: Noble gases are inert (unreactive) due to stable electron configuration. Highly reactive is NOT their property.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about catalysts?
questions.explanation: Catalysts do not change equilibrium constant (thermodynamic property). They only help reach equilibrium faster by lowering activation energy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of ionic compounds?
questions.explanation: Ionic compounds are soluble in polar solvents (like water) due to ion-dipole interactions. They are generally insoluble in non-polar solvents like benzene, CCl₄.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of covalent compounds?
questions.explanation: Covalent compounds do not conduct electricity in molten state as they lack free ions. Ionic compounds conduct electricity when molten or in solution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT exhibit hydrogen bonding?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding requires H bonded to highly electronegative atom (F, O, N). In CH₄, H is bonded to C (low electronegativity), so no hydrogen bonding.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules is NOT polar?
questions.explanation: CO₂ is linear (O=C=O) with symmetrical structure, bond dipoles cancel, giving zero net dipole moment. Others have unsymmetrical geometry with net dipole.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT have a linear geometry?
questions.explanation: H₂O has bent geometry (bond angle 104.5°) due to two lone pairs on oxygen. CO₂, BeCl₂, and C₂H₂ (acetylene) are linear molecules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a consequence of hydrogen bonding?

options.D: High solubility of NH₃ in water

questions.explanation: Low boiling point of H₂S is due to absence of hydrogen bonding (S less electronegative). High boiling point of water, ice floating, and NH₃ solubility are consequences of H-bonding.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT have sp³ hybridization?
questions.explanation: CO₂ has sp hybridization (linear). CH₄, NH₃, and H₂O all have sp³ hybridization (tetrahedral, pyramidal, bent respectively).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT follow Markovnikov's rule?

options.B: CH₃–CH=CH₂ + HBr in presence of peroxide

questions.explanation: Peroxide effect causes anti-Markovnikov addition (free radical mechanism). HBr adds with Br on less substituted carbon. Others follow Markovnikov rule.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following reactions does NOT give an alkene as major product?
questions.explanation: Hydrogenation of alkyne gives alkane (with excess H₂) or alkene (with limited H₂). The question asks for NOT giving alkene - complete hydrogenation gives alkane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds does NOT exhibit geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: 1-butene (CH₂=CH–CH₂–CH₃) has one carbon of double bond with two H atoms, so no geometrical isomerism. Others have different groups on each doubly bonded carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a periodic property?
questions.explanation: Density is not strictly periodic as it depends on atomic mass and atomic volume, which do not show regular periodic trends. Atomic radius, IE, and EN show clear periodic trends.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correct about bond order?

options.A: Bond order is inversely proportional to bond length
options.B: Bond order is directly proportional to bond strength

questions.explanation: Bond order of O₂ is 2 (from MO theory: (10-6)/2 = 2). It is paramagnetic with bond order 2, not 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about ionization energy?

options.C: Be has higher IE than B
options.D: N has lower IE than O

questions.explanation: N has higher IE than O due to half-filled stability (2p³ configuration). Removing electron from O (2p⁴) is easier. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about electron affinity?

options.A: Electron affinity of Cl is highest
options.B: Electron affinity of F is lower than Cl
options.C: Electron affinity decreases down a group
options.D: Electron affinity of noble gases is high

questions.explanation: Noble gases have very low (almost zero or negative) electron affinity due to stable electron configurations. They have no tendency to accept electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a d-block element?
questions.explanation: Al is p-block element (group 13). Fe, Cu, and Zn are d-block elements (transition series).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about hydrogen bonding?

options.A: It is stronger than covalent bond
options.D: It occurs in HF, H₂O, NH₃

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is much weaker than covalent bonds (about 5-10% of covalent bond strength). Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT have a linear structure?
questions.explanation: SO₂ has bent structure due to lone pair on S. I₃⁻, XeF₂, and CO₂ are all linear molecules with 180° bond angle.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of metallic bonding?
questions.explanation: Metallic bonds are non-directional (electron sea model). Directional bonds are characteristic of covalent bonding. Malleability and ductility are properties of metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following trends is INCORRECT for group 17 elements?

options.A: Atomic radius increases down the group
options.B: Ionization energy decreases down the group
options.C: Electron affinity decreases down the group
