options.D: Oxidizing power increases down the group

questions.explanation: Oxidizing power decreases down the group (F₂ is strongest oxidizing agent, I₂ is weakest). Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about transition metals?
questions.explanation: Many transition metals and their ions have unpaired electrons and are paramagnetic. Diamagnetic means all electrons paired, which is not characteristic of transition metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a lanthanoid?
questions.explanation: Actinium (Ac, Z=89) is an actinoid, not a lanthanoid. Lanthanoids are elements from Ce (58) to Lu (71).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about ionization energy is INCORRECT?

options.A: IE₁ of Na is less than IE₁ of Mg
options.B: IE₁ of Mg is greater than IE₁ of Al
options.C: IE₁ of N is greater than IE₁ of O
options.D: IE₁ of O is greater than IE₁ of F

questions.explanation: IE₁ of O (1314 kJ/mol) is less than IE₁ of F (1681 kJ/mol). F has higher nuclear charge and smaller size, so higher IE. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about ionic bonds?

options.A: Formed by complete transfer of electrons

questions.explanation: Ionic compounds do not conduct electricity in solid state because ions are fixed in lattice and cannot move. They conduct only in molten or aqueous state.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct statement about bond length?

options.A: Triple bond < Double bond < Single bond
options.B: Bond length increases with bond order
options.C: Bond length increases with atomic size
options.D: Bond length decreases with multiple bonding

questions.explanation: Bond length decreases with increasing bond order (more shared electrons pull nuclei closer). Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT decolourise bromine water?
questions.explanation: Benzene does not decolourise bromine water under normal conditions as it undergoes substitution, not addition. Alkenes and alkynes add bromine, decolourising it.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct method to distinguish between 1-butyne and 2-butyne?
questions.explanation: Both 1-butyne and 2-butyne decolourise Br₂ water (both have triple bond). Tollen's test, NaNH₂, and ammoniacal AgNO₃ react only with terminal alkynes (1-butyne).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following trends is INCORRECT for halogens?

options.A: Atomic radius: I > Br > Cl > F
options.B: Bond dissociation energy: Cl₂ > Br₂ > F₂ > I₂
options.C: Oxidizing power: F₂ > Cl₂ > Br₂ > I₂
options.D: Electron affinity: F > Cl > Br > I

questions.explanation: Electron affinity order: Cl > F > Br > I. F has unexpectedly low electron affinity due to small size and electron repulsion. Statement is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): BF₃ behaves as a Lewis acid. Reason (R): Boron in BF₃ has an incomplete octet and can accept a pair of electrons.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: BF₃ is electron deficient (boron has only 6 electrons in valence shell). It acts as Lewis acid by accepting electron pair from Lewis base. The reason correctly explains the assertion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): H₂O has higher boiling point than H₂S. Reason (R): Water molecules form intermolecular hydrogen bonding.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Water (H₂O) has higher boiling point due to strong intermolecular hydrogen bonding. H₂S has weaker van der Waals forces. The reason correctly explains the assertion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): NH₃ has higher dipole moment than NF₃. Reason (R): In NH₃, bond dipole and lone pair dipole are in same direction; in NF₃, they are in opposite direction.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: In NH₃, N–H bond dipoles add to lone pair dipole. In NF₃, N–F bond dipoles oppose lone pair dipole. Resultant dipole moment of NH₃ (1.47 D) > NF₃ (0.24 D). Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): CO₂ is non-polar while SO₂ is polar. Reason (R): CO₂ is linear while SO₂ is bent in shape.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: CO₂ has linear geometry (O=C=O) with bond dipoles canceling. SO₂ has bent geometry (bond angle ~119°) with net dipole moment. The reason correctly explains the polarity difference.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Bond angle in H₂O is less than that in NH₃. Reason (R): Lone pair-lone pair repulsion is stronger than lone pair-bond pair repulsion.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: H₂O has two lone pairs (more lp-lp repulsion) with bond angle 104.5°. NH₃ has one lone pair (less repulsion) with bond angle 107.5°. The reason correctly explains the bond angle difference.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): CCl₄ is non-polar while CHCl₃ is polar. Reason (R): CCl₄ has symmetrical tetrahedral structure while CHCl₃ is unsymmetrical.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: CCl₄ has all four bond dipoles canceling due to symmetry. CHCl₃ has unsymmetrical structure (three C–Cl and one C–H) giving net dipole. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): O₃ molecule is bent while CO₂ is linear. Reason (R): Ozone has a lone pair on central atom while CO₂ has no lone pair on carbon.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Ozone (O₃) has bent structure (bond angle 117°) due to lone pair on central O. CO₂ has linear structure due to sp hybridization and no lone pair on C. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): For an exothermic reaction, equilibrium constant decreases with increase in temperature. Reason (R): Increase in temperature favours endothermic reactions.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: For exothermic reactions (ΔH < 0), increasing temperature shifts equilibrium backward (Le Chatelier's principle), decreasing K. The reason correctly explains this behavior.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): ΔG for a spontaneous process is always negative. Reason (R): ΔG = ΔH – TΔS, and for spontaneity, the free energy of system decreases.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both statements are true, but the reason (ΔG = ΔH – TΔS) is the definition, not the explanation of why ΔG must be negative for spontaneity. The explanation is that systems tend toward minimum free energy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): The entropy of the universe always increases in a spontaneous process. Reason (R): Spontaneous processes are irreversible and lead to increase in randomness.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Second law of thermodynamics states that total entropy of universe increases in spontaneous (irreversible) processes due to increased randomness. Reason correctly explains the assertion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): For an endothermic reaction, increasing temperature increases the equilibrium constant. Reason (R): According to Le Chatelier's principle, increasing temperature favours the endothermic direction.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: For endothermic reactions (ΔH > 0), increasing temperature shifts equilibrium forward, increasing K. Le Chatelier's principle correctly explains this.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Melting of ice is spontaneous at 10°C but not at –10°C. Reason (R): ΔG = ΔH – TΔS; at low temperatures, TΔS term is small and ΔG becomes positive.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Melting is endothermic (ΔH > 0) with ΔS > 0. At T > 0°C, TΔS > ΔH, ΔG negative. At T < 0°C, TΔS < ΔH, ΔG positive. The thermodynamic equation correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): In the reaction N₂(g) + 3H₂(g) ⇌ 2NH₃(g), increasing pressure favours forward reaction. Reason (R): Forward reaction proceeds with decrease in number of moles.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: According to Le Chatelier's principle, increasing pressure favours side with fewer gas moles. Forward reaction has 2 moles (NH₃) vs 4 moles (N₂+3H₂) reactants. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): For the reaction 2HI(g) ⇌ H₂(g) + I₂(g), Kp = Kc. Reason (R): For this reaction, Δn = 0 (number of moles of gaseous products equals reactants).

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. When Δn = 0, Kp = Kc. For 2HI ⇌ H₂ + I₂, products (2 moles) and reactants (2 moles) have equal gas moles, so Δn = 0. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Adding an inert gas at constant volume does not affect equilibrium. Reason (R): Partial pressures of reactants and products remain unchanged.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: At constant volume, adding inert gas increases total pressure but partial pressures of reactants/products remain constant (concentrations unchanged), so equilibrium unaffected. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): A catalyst does not change the equilibrium constant. Reason (R): Catalyst lowers activation energy for both forward and reverse reactions equally.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both statements are true, but the reason (lowering activation energy equally) explains why catalyst increases rate, not why K remains constant. K remains constant because ΔG° (thermodynamic property) is unchanged.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): For the reaction CaCO₃(s) ⇌ CaO(s) + CO₂(g), Kp = pCO₂. Reason (R): Equilibrium constant expressions include only gaseous species; solids have constant activity = 1.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: For heterogeneous equilibria, solids have constant activity (taken as 1). So Kp = pCO₂ only. The reason correctly explains why only CO₂ appears in expression.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Copper can displace silver from silver nitrate solution. Reason (R): Standard electrode potential of Cu²⁺/Cu is less than that of Ag⁺/Ag.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Cu (E° = +0.34 V) has lower reduction potential than Ag (E° = +0.80 V), so Cu can reduce Ag⁺ to Ag. The reason correctly explains the displacement reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Standard electrode potential of Zn²⁺/Zn is negative while that of Cu²⁺/Cu is positive. Reason (R): Zinc has higher tendency to lose electrons than copper.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: More negative E° indicates stronger reducing agent (greater tendency to lose electrons). Zn (E° = –0.76 V) loses electrons more readily than Cu (E° = +0.34 V). Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) In the electrolysis of aqueous NaCl, H₂ is liberated at cathode instead of Na. Reason (R):) Standard electrode potential of H⁺/H₂ is less negative than that of Na⁺/Na.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: H⁺/H₂ (E° = 0.00 V) has higher reduction potential than Na⁺/Na (E° = –2.71 V), so H⁺ is reduced preferentially at cathode. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Standard electrode potential of fluorine is the highest among halogens. Reason (R):) Fluorine has the highest electronegativity.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both statements true, but high electronegativity relates to electron affinity, not directly to electrode potential. High E° for F₂ is due to low bond dissociation energy and high hydration energy of F⁻.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) The emf of a Daniel cell decreases with increase in concentration of Zn²⁺ ions. Reason (R):) According to Nernst equation, E = E° – (RT/nF) ln([Zn²⁺]/[Cu²⁺]).

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Nernst equation shows that increasing [Zn²⁺] increases the reaction quotient Q, making log term more positive, thus E decreases. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) In a galvanic cell, electrons flow from anode to cathode through external circuit. Reason (R):) Anode has higher negative potential and undergoes oxidation.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Anode (oxidation) has more negative potential, so electrons are released and flow to cathode (reduction) through external circuit. Reason correctly explains electron flow direction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Nitrogen does not form pentahalides. Reason (R):) Nitrogen lacks d-orbitals to expand its octet.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Nitrogen (second period) has no d-orbitals, cannot expand octet beyond 8 electrons, so cannot form NF₅. Phosphorus (third period) has d-orbitals and forms PF₅. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Ozone is a powerful oxidizing agent. Reason (R):) Ozone decomposes to give nascent oxygen.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Ozone (O₃) decomposes to O₂ and nascent oxygen [O], which is highly reactive and acts as strong oxidizing agent. Reason correctly explains the oxidizing property.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) HClO₄ is a stronger acid than HClO₃. Reason (R):) Higher oxidation state of Cl leads to greater stabilization of conjugate base.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: HClO₄ has Cl in +7 oxidation state, HClO₃ has +5. Higher oxidation state withdraws electrons more strongly, stabilizing ClO₄⁻ conjugate base, making acid stronger. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) White phosphorus is more reactive than red phosphorus. Reason (R):) White phosphorus has P₄ tetrahedral structure with strained bonds.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: White phosphorus (P₄) has tetrahedral structure with bond angles 60°, causing bond strain and high reactivity. Red phosphorus has polymeric structure with less strain. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) NH₃ has higher boiling point than PH₃. Reason (R):) NH₃ molecules form intermolecular hydrogen bonding.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: NH₃ has strong intermolecular hydrogen bonding due to high electronegativity of N and small size. PH₃ has only weak van der Waals forces. Reason correctly explains higher boiling point.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Sulphur dioxide decolourizes acidified KMnO₄ solution. Reason (R):) Sulphur dioxide acts as a reducing agent.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: SO₂ acts as reducing agent, reduces purple MnO₄⁻ to colourless Mn²⁺. The reducing property of SO₂ correctly explains the decolourization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Boron in BF₃ is sp² hybridized. Reason (R):) BF₃ has trigonal planar geometry.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both statements true, but geometry is consequence of hybridization, not explanation. sp² hybridization leads to trigonal planar geometry, not vice versa.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Silicones are water repellent. Reason (R):) Silicones have organic groups attached to Si–O–Si backbone.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Silicones have hydrophobic organic groups (methyl, phenyl) attached to inorganic siloxane backbone, making them water repellent. Reason correctly explains the property.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Carbon dioxide is a gas while silicon dioxide is a solid. Reason (R):) CO₂ is molecular while SiO₂ is a three-dimensional network solid.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: CO₂ forms discrete molecules (weak intermolecular forces, gas). SiO₂ forms giant covalent network (strong bonds throughout, solid). Reason correctly explains physical state difference.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Dipole moment of NH₃ is greater than that of NF₃. Statement II: Nitrogen is more electronegative than fluorine.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Statement I is true (NH₃ dipole 1.47 D > NF₃ 0.24 D). Statement II is false: Fluorine (4.0) is more electronegative than nitrogen (3.0).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Bond angle in H₂O is less than that in NH₃. Statement II: Oxygen has higher electronegativity than nitrogen.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Statement I true (104.5° < 107.5°). Statement II false: Oxygen (3.5) is more electronegative than nitrogen (3.0), but bond angle difference is due to lone pair repulsion, not electronegativity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: XeF₄ has square planar geometry. Statement II: Xe in XeF₄ is sp³d² hybridized.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: XeF₄ has 4 bond pairs and 2 lone pairs, sp³d² hybridization with square planar geometry (lone pairs occupy axial positions). Both statements correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: H₂S has lower boiling point than H₂O. Statement II: H₂S has weaker hydrogen bonding than H₂O.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: H₂O has strong intermolecular H-bonding (high boiling point). H₂S has very weak H-bonding (S less electronegative), so lower boiling point. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: All spontaneous processes are exothermic. Statement II: For spontaneous processes, ΔG is always negative.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Statement I false: Many endothermic processes (melting of ice at >0°C) are spontaneous. Statement II true: ΔG < 0 is the criterion for spontaneity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Entropy of a perfectly crystalline substance is zero at absolute zero. Statement II: Third law of thermodynamics states that entropy of all substances is zero at 0 K.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Statement I true: Third law states entropy of perfectly crystalline substance is zero at 0 K. Statement II false: It applies only to perfectly crystalline substances, not all substances.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: ΔH of formation of a compound can be zero. Statement II: ΔH of formation of an element in its standard state is zero.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Statement I true: For elements in standard state, ΔHf° = 0. Statement II correctly states this convention.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: A catalyst increases the rate of forward and backward reactions equally. Statement II: Catalyst does not change the equilibrium constant.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Catalyst lowers activation energy for both directions equally, increasing both rates. Equilibrium constant (thermodynamic property) remains unchanged. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: For the reaction N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃, Kp = Kc(RT)⁻². Statement II: Δn for this reaction is –2.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. Δn = 2 – 4 = –2, so Kp = Kc(RT)⁻². Both statements correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: In the reaction 2HI ⇌ H₂ + I₂, total number of moles at equilibrium remains constant. Statement II: This reaction proceeds with no change in number of moles.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Reaction has 2 moles reactants and 2 moles products, so Δn = 0, total moles constant. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Standard electrode potential of hydrogen electrode is taken as zero. Statement II: Hydrogen is the most reactive element.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Statement I true: SHE is reference electrode with E° = 0 by convention. Statement II false: Fluorine is more reactive; hydrogen is not the most reactive element.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Electrolysis of molten NaCl gives Na at cathode and Cl₂ at anode. Statement II: During electrolysis, cations migrate to cathode and anions to anode.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: In molten NaCl electrolysis, Na⁺ reduced at cathode (Na), Cl⁻ oxidized at anode (Cl₂). Cations move to cathode, anions to anode. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Conductivity of electrolytic solutions increases with dilution. Statement II: Number of ions per unit volume increases on dilution.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Statement I true: Conductivity increases due to increased ionic mobility. Statement II false: Number of ions per unit volume actually decreases on dilution, but mobility increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Nitrogen is chemically inert at room temperature. Statement II: N₂ molecule has high bond dissociation energy.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: N₂ has triple bond (N≡N) with bond energy 946 kJ/mol, making it kinetically inert at room temperature. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: White phosphorus is stored under water. Statement II: White phosphorus is highly reactive and ignites in air.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: White phosphorus is highly reactive, ignites spontaneously in air, so stored under water to prevent contact with air. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Carbon shows catenation property to maximum extent. Statement II: C–C bond strength is very high.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Carbon exhibits maximum catenation due to high C–C bond strength (high bond energy). Both statements true and related.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: HClO₄ is stronger acid than H₂SO₄. Statement II: Chlorine has higher electronegativity than sulfur.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: HClO₄ is strongest oxyacid due to high electronegativity of Cl (+7 oxidation state). Cl (3.2) is more electronegative than S (2.6). Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity. Statement II: In graphite, each carbon has one free electron that is delocalized.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: In graphite, each carbon is sp² hybridized with one delocalized π electron that conducts electricity along layers. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Bond angle decreases with increasing number of lone pairs on central atom. Explanation: Lone pair-lone pair repulsion > lone pair-bond pair repulsion > bond pair-bond pair repulsion.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: The VSEPR theory states that lone pairs occupy more space and cause greater repulsion, reducing bond angles. The explanation correctly justifies the principle.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Molecules with identical atoms and symmetrical geometry are non-polar. Explanation: In symmetrical molecules, bond dipoles cancel each other.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Symmetrical molecules like CO₂, CCl₄ have bond dipoles that cancel vectorially, resulting in zero net dipole moment. Explanation correctly justifies principle.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: d-orbital participation in bonding increases down a group. Explanation: Availability of d-orbitals increases as principal quantum number increases.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Higher elements (P, S, Cl) have vacant d-orbitals available for bonding, while second period elements (N, O, F) lack d-orbitals. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Exothermic reactions with increase in entropy are spontaneous at all temperatures. Explanation: For such reactions, ΔG = ΔH – TΔS is always negative.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: When ΔH < 0 and ΔS > 0, ΔG = negative – T(positive) = negative at all T. The thermodynamic equation correctly justifies the principle.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: ΔG for a process is zero at equilibrium. Explanation: At equilibrium, forward and reverse rates are equal.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Both statements true, but equal rates (kinetic) does not directly explain ΔG = 0 (thermodynamic). ΔG = 0 at equilibrium because system has minimum free energy. NEET Chemistry Practice Set 7 - Concept Guru Special (Assertion-Reason, Statement I & II, Principle-Explanation)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Standard electrode potential of Zn²⁺/Zn is negative while that of Cu²⁺/Cu is positive. Reason (R): Zinc has higher tendency to lose electrons than copper.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: More negative E° indicates stronger reducing agent (greater tendency to lose electrons). Zn (E° = –0.76 V) loses electrons more readily than Cu (E° = +0.34 V). Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): In the electrolysis of aqueous NaCl, H₂ is liberated at cathode instead of Na. Reason (R): Standard electrode potential of H⁺/H₂ is less negative than that of Na⁺/Na.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: H⁺/H₂ (E° = 0.00 V) has higher reduction potential than Na⁺/Na (E° = –2.71 V), so H⁺ is reduced preferentially at cathode. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Standard electrode potential of fluorine is the highest among halogens. Reason (R): Fluorine has the highest electronegativity.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both statements true, but high electronegativity relates to electron affinity, not directly to electrode potential. High E° for F₂ is due to low bond dissociation energy and high hydration energy of F⁻.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): The emf of a Daniel cell decreases with increase in concentration of Zn²⁺ ions. Reason (R): According to Nernst equation, E = E° – (RT/nF) ln([Zn²⁺]/[Cu²⁺]).

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Nernst equation shows that increasing [Zn²⁺] increases the reaction quotient Q, making log term more positive, thus E decreases. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a galvanic cell, electrons flow from anode to cathode through external circuit. Reason (R): Anode has higher negative potential and undergoes oxidation.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Anode (oxidation) has more negative potential, so electrons are released and flow to cathode (reduction) through external circuit. Reason correctly explains electron flow direction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Nitrogen does not form pentahalides. Reason (R): Nitrogen lacks d-orbitals to expand its octet.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Nitrogen (second period) has no d-orbitals, cannot expand octet beyond 8 electrons, so cannot form NF₅. Phosphorus (third period) has d-orbitals and forms PF₅. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Ozone is a powerful oxidizing agent. Reason (R): Ozone decomposes to give nascent oxygen.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Ozone (O₃) decomposes to O₂ and nascent oxygen [O], which is highly reactive and acts as strong oxidizing agent. Reason correctly explains the oxidizing property.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): HClO₄ is a stronger acid than HClO₃. Reason (R): Higher oxidation state of Cl leads to greater stabilization of conjugate base.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: HClO₄ has Cl in +7 oxidation state, HClO₃ has +5. Higher oxidation state withdraws electrons more strongly, stabilizing ClO₄⁻ conjugate base, making acid stronger. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): White phosphorus is more reactive than red phosphorus. Reason (R): White phosphorus has P₄ tetrahedral structure with strained bonds.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: White phosphorus (P₄) has tetrahedral structure with bond angles 60°, causing bond strain and high reactivity. Red phosphorus has polymeric structure with less strain. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): NH₃ has higher boiling point than PH₃. Reason (R): NH₃ molecules form intermolecular hydrogen bonding.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: NH₃ has strong intermolecular hydrogen bonding due to high electronegativity of N and small size. PH₃ has only weak van der Waals forces. Reason correctly explains higher boiling point.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Sulphur dioxide decolourizes acidified KMnO₄ solution. Reason (R): Sulphur dioxide acts as a reducing agent.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: SO₂ acts as reducing agent, reduces purple MnO₄⁻ to colourless Mn²⁺. The reducing property of SO₂ correctly explains the decolourization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Boron in BF₃ is sp² hybridized. Reason (R): BF₃ has trigonal planar geometry.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both statements true, but geometry is consequence of hybridization, not explanation. sp² hybridization leads to trigonal planar geometry, not vice versa.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Silicones are water repellent. Reason (R): Silicones have organic groups attached to Si–O–Si backbone.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Silicones have hydrophobic organic groups (methyl, phenyl) attached to inorganic siloxane backbone, making them water repellent. Reason correctly explains the property.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Carbon dioxide is a gas while silicon dioxide is a solid. Reason (R): CO₂ is molecular while SiO₂ is a three-dimensional network solid.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: CO₂ forms discrete molecules (weak intermolecular forces, gas). SiO₂ forms giant covalent network (strong bonds throughout, solid). Reason correctly explains physical state difference.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Increasing pressure favours reaction that produces fewer gas molecules. Explanation: Pressure increase shifts equilibrium to side with smaller number of moles to reduce pressure.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Le Chatelier's principle states that increasing pressure favours side with fewer gas moles to counteract the pressure increase. Explanation correctly justifies the principle.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Adding a catalyst does not affect the equilibrium position. Explanation: Catalyst lowers activation energy for both forward and reverse reactions equally.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: By lowering activation energy equally for both directions, catalyst increases both rates equally, so equilibrium position (ratio of concentrations) remains unchanged. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: More reactive metals have more negative standard electrode potentials. Explanation: More negative E° indicates greater tendency to lose electrons (oxidize).

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Standard electrode potential measures tendency to gain electrons. More negative E° means less tendency to gain electrons = more tendency to lose electrons = more reactive as reducing agent. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Conductivity of strong electrolytes decreases slightly with dilution. Explanation: Number of ions per unit volume decreases on dilution.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: For strong electrolytes, conductivity decreases slightly on dilution because while ionic mobility increases, the number of ions per unit volume decreases more significantly. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Reducing character of hydrides of group 15 increases down the group. Explanation: Bond dissociation energy of E–H bond decreases down the group.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: F₂ has unexpectedly low bond dissociation energy due to strong electron-electron repulsion between lone pairs on adjacent small F atoms. Cl₂ has larger size, less repulsion, higher bond energy. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Bond angle in H₂O is less than ideal tetrahedral angle. Reason (R): Lone pair-lone pair repulsion compresses the bond angle.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: H₂O has sp³ hybridization (ideal angle 109.5°), but two lone pairs cause strong lp-lp repulsion, reducing bond angle to 104.5°. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): NH₃ has higher boiling point than PH₃. Reason (R): NH₃ forms intermolecular hydrogen bonds.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: NH₃ molecules form strong intermolecular H-bonds due to high electronegativity of N. PH₃ has only weak van der Waals forces. Reason correctly explains higher boiling point.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): ICl is more polar than I₂. Reason (R): ICl has electronegativity difference between I and Cl.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: I₂ is non-polar (identical atoms, zero dipole). ICl has different atoms with electronegativity difference, creating bond dipole. Reason correctly explains polarity difference.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): BeCl₂ is linear while SnCl₂ is angular. Reason (R):) Be has no lone pair while Sn has one lone pair.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Be in BeCl₂ has no lone pair (sp hybridization, linear). Sn in SnCl₂ has one lone pair (sp² hybridization, bent geometry due to lp-bp repulsion). Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): BF₃ is planar while NF₃ is pyramidal. Reason (R): B has no lone pair while N has one lone pair.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: BF₃ has sp² hybridization (no lone pair, trigonal planar). NF₃ has sp³ hybridization with one lone pair (pyramidal geometry). Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): SO₂ is polar while CO₂ is non-polar. Reason (R): SO₂ has bent shape while CO₂ is linear.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: SO₂ has bent geometry due to lone pair on S, giving net dipole. CO₂ is linear with canceling dipoles. Shape difference correctly explains polarity difference.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Bond length in O₂⁺ is shorter than in O₂. Reason (R): Bond order of O₂⁺ is higher than that of O₂.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: O₂ has bond order 2, O₂⁺ has bond order 2.5 (removing electron from antibonding orbital). Higher bond order means shorter bond length. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): N₂ is diamagnetic while O₂ is paramagnetic. Reason (R): N₂ has all electrons paired while O₂ has two unpaired electrons.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Molecular orbital configuration: N₂ has all electrons paired (diamagnetic). O₂ has two unpaired electrons in π* orbitals (paramagnetic). Reason correctly explains magnetic behavior.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Enthalpy of formation of H₂O(l) is less than that of H₂O(g). Reason (R): Condensation of vapour to liquid releases heat.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: ΔHf° of H₂O(l) = –285.8 kJ/mol, H₂O(g) = –241.8 kJ/mol. Liquid form has more negative value because condensation releases extra heat. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Internal energy change (ΔU) for a reaction is measured in a bomb calorimeter. Reason (R): Bomb calorimeter maintains constant volume.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: At constant volume, heat absorbed = ΔU. Bomb calorimeter is a constant volume apparatus, so it measures ΔU directly. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): ΔH for a reaction can be calculated using Hess's law. Reason (R):) Enthalpy is a state function.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Hess's law states that enthalpy change depends only on initial and final states (path independent) because enthalpy is a state function. Reason correctly explains why Hess's law works.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): For an ideal gas, Cp – Cv = R. Reason (R): For ideal gas, (∂U/∂V)T = 0.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both statements true, but (∂U/∂V)T = 0 (internal energy independent of volume) is a property of ideal gas, not direct explanation of Cp – Cv = R. The relation comes from enthalpy definition.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): At constant temperature, ΔG = ΔH – TΔS. Reason (R): Gibbs free energy is defined as G = H – TS.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: The equation ΔG = ΔH – TΔS at constant T follows directly from the definition G = H – TS. Reason correctly explains the origin of the equation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Spontaneous processes are always exothermic. Reason (R): For spontaneous processes, ΔG is negative.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Assertion false: Many spontaneous processes are endothermic (melting ice at >0°C). Reason true: ΔG < 0 is correct criterion for spontaneity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): In the reaction N₂ + O₂ ⇌ 2NO, increasing pressure does not affect equilibrium. Reason (R): The reaction has Δn = 0 (no change in number of moles).

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: When Δn = 0, pressure change affects both sides equally, so equilibrium position unchanged. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Kp = Kc for the reaction H₂ + I₂ ⇌ 2HI. Reason (R): For this reaction, Δn = 0.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. When Δn = 0, (RT)^0 = 1, so Kp = Kc. Reason correctly explains the equality.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Equilibrium constant changes with temperature. Reason (R):) ΔG° = –RT ln K, and ΔG° depends on temperature.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: ΔG° = ΔH° – TΔS°. As T changes, ΔG° changes, so K must change to maintain the relation ΔG° = –RT ln K. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): A catalyst increases the rate of forward reaction. Reason (R): Catalyst lowers the activation energy.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: According to Arrhenius theory, rate constant k = Ae⁻ᴱᵃ/ᴿᵀ. Lowering Ea increases k, thus increases reaction rate. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): For the reaction CaCO₃(s) ⇌ CaO(s) + CO₂(g), Kp = pCO₂. Reason (R): Solids have constant activity (taken as 1) in equilibrium expression.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: For heterogeneous equilibria, concentration of pure solids is constant and incorporated into K, leaving only gaseous species in expression. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Standard electrode potential of Zn²⁺/Zn is –0.76 V. Reason (R): Zinc has high tendency to lose electrons.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both true, but the reason (tendency to lose electrons) explains why the value is negative, not the specific numerical value. The numerical value comes from experimental measurement against SHE.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) In a concentration cell, Ecell becomes zero when concentrations become equal. Reason (R): At equal concentrations, ΔG = 0.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: For concentration cell, E = (RT/nF) ln(C₂/C₁). When C₁ = C₂, ln 1 = 0, so E = 0. Also ΔG = –nFE = 0 at equilibrium. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Electrolysis of aqueous CuSO₄ with Cu electrodes results in transfer of Cu from anode to cathode. Reason (R): At anode, Cu dissolves; at cathode, Cu deposits.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: With active Cu electrodes, anode reaction: Cu → Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻; cathode reaction: Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu. Cu is transferred from anode to cathode. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Conductivity of electrolytes increases with temperature. Reason (R): Ionic mobility increases with temperature.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Increasing temperature decreases viscosity and increases ionic mobility, allowing ions to move faster, increasing conductivity. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Faraday's laws of electrolysis are based on conservation of charge. Reason (R): Amount of substance deposited is proportional to quantity of electricity passed.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both true, but the reason (proportionality) is Faraday's first law itself, not the explanation of why the law is based on charge conservation. The underlying principle is that each electron transfers one charge unit.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Boron in B₂H₆ is sp³ hybridized. Reason (R): B₂H₆ contains three-center two-electron bonds.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Both true, but the three-center bonds (banana bonds) are a consequence of sp³ hybridization with electron deficiency, not the explanation of why B is sp³ hybridized.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) HNO₃ acts as an oxidizing agent. Reason (R): Nitrogen in HNO₃ is in +5 oxidation state and can be reduced.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Highest oxidation state of N (+5) means it can accept electrons (be reduced) to lower oxidation states, acting as oxidizing agent. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) SO₂ acts as both oxidizing and reducing agent. Reason (R): Sulfur in SO₂ is in +4 oxidation state, which can increase to +6 or decrease to lower states.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: +4 oxidation state of S is intermediate: can be oxidized to +6 (reducing agent) or reduced to 0, -2 (oxidizing agent). Reason correctly explains dual behavior.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Carbon shows maximum catenation in group 14. Reason (R): C–C bond energy is very high.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Catenation tendency depends on bond strength. C–C bond (348 kJ/mol) is strongest among group 14 elements, allowing carbon to form long chains. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) White phosphorus is stored under water. Reason (R): White phosphorus is highly reactive and ignites in air.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: White phosphorus has low ignition temperature and spontaneously ignites in air, so stored under water to prevent contact with oxygen. Reason correctly explains storage method.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Noble gases have very low boiling points. Reason (R): Noble gases have weak dispersion forces between atoms.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Noble gases are monatomic with only weak London dispersion forces (increasing with atomic size). Weak intermolecular forces explain low boiling points. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A):) Xe forms compounds with F and O but not with He and Ne. Reason (R): He and Ne have very high ionization energies.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Noble gas reactivity depends on ionization energy. He and Ne have very high IE (too high to form compounds). Xe has lowest IE among noble gases, can form compounds with highly electronegative F and O. Reason correctly explains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: BF₃ is an electron deficient compound. Statement II: Boron in BF₃ has only six electrons in its valence shell.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: BF₃ has only 6 electrons around B (3 bond pairs), making it electron deficient. Both statements are true and related.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Dipole moment of CH₃Cl is greater than that of CHCl₃. Statement II: In CHCl₃, bond dipoles partially cancel each other.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: CH₃Cl (μ = 1.87 D) has higher dipole than CHCl₃ (μ = 1.01 D). In CHCl₃, three C–Cl dipoles partially cancel, giving lower net dipole. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Bond order of N₂ is 3. Statement II: N₂ molecule has one sigma and two pi bonds.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: N₂ has bond order 3 (triple bond) consisting of one σ and two π bonds. Both statements are true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: O₂ is paramagnetic. Statement II: O₂ has two unpaired electrons in its molecular orbitals.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Molecular orbital configuration of O₂ shows two unpaired electrons in π* orbitals, making it paramagnetic. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Hess's law is based on enthalpy being a state function. Statement II: Enthalpy change depends only on initial and final states.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Hess's law (enthalpy change independent of path) follows directly from enthalpy being a state function (depends only on initial and final states). Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: ΔG for a spontaneous process is negative. Statement II: Spontaneous processes occur without external assistance.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: ΔG < 0 is thermodynamic criterion for spontaneity. Spontaneous processes are those that occur naturally without external intervention. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Entropy of a substance increases with temperature. Statement II: At higher temperature, molecular randomness increases.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: As temperature increases, molecular motion increases, randomness increases, so entropy increases. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: For the reaction N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃, Kp < Kc. Statement II: For this reaction, Δn is negative.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. Δn = –2, so (RT)^–2 < 1, therefore Kp < Kc. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Adding inert gas at constant volume does not affect equilibrium. Statement II: Partial pressures of reactants and products remain unchanged.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: At constant volume, adding inert gas increases total pressure but partial pressures (and concentrations) of reacting species remain constant, so equilibrium unaffected. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Standard electrode potential of Zn²⁺/Zn is –0.76 V. Statement II: Zinc is placed above hydrogen in reactivity series.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Zn has negative E° (–0.76 V) meaning it can displace H⁺ from acids, so placed above hydrogen in reactivity series. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: In electrolytic cell, anode is positive electrode. Statement II: Oxidation occurs at anode.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: In electrolytic cell, anode is connected to positive terminal of battery and oxidation occurs at anode. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Conductivity decreases with dilution for strong electrolytes. Statement II: Number of ions per unit volume decreases on dilution.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: On dilution, number of ions per unit volume decreases, so conductivity (κ) decreases. Molar conductivity increases due to increased ionic mobility. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Diamond is a bad conductor of electricity. Statement II: In diamond, all electrons are localized in covalent bonds.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Diamond has no free electrons (all four electrons of each C atom in covalent bonds), so it is a bad conductor. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity. Statement II: In graphite, one electron per carbon is delocalized.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: In graphite, each C is sp² hybridized with one delocalized π electron that moves freely along layers, conducting electricity. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Nitrogen does not form pentachloride. Statement II: Nitrogen lacks d-orbitals to expand its octet.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: NCl₅ cannot exist because N (second period) has no d-orbitals to accommodate more than 8 electrons. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: Ozone is a powerful oxidizing agent. Statement II: Ozone decomposes to give nascent oxygen.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: O₃ → O₂ + [O]; nascent oxygen is highly reactive and acts as strong oxidizing agent. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: SO₂ acts as a reducing agent. Statement II: SO₂ can be oxidized to SO₃.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: SO₂ (S in +4 state) can be oxidized to SO₃ (S in +6 state), so it acts as reducing agent. Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Statement I: All C–C bonds in benzene are equal. Statement II: Benzene exhibits resonance.

options.A: Both Statement I and II are true
options.B: Both Statement I and II are false
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Due to resonance, all C–C bonds in benzene have equal length (intermediate between single and double bond). Both statements true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Bond length decreases as bond order increases. Explanation: Higher bond order means more shared electrons pulling nuclei closer.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Multiple bonds (higher bond order) have greater electron density between nuclei, increasing attraction and decreasing bond length. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Bond angle decreases with increasing electronegativity of surrounding atoms. Explanation: More electronegative atoms pull bond pairs closer, reducing repulsion.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Example: In NH₃, bond angle 107.5°; in NF₃, bond angle 102.5°. F more electronegative pulls bond pairs away from N, reducing bp-bp repulsion. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Molecules with even number of electrons may still be paramagnetic. Explanation: Electrons may occupy degenerate orbitals with parallel spins (Hund's rule).

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: O₂ has 12 valence electrons but is paramagnetic because electrons occupy π* orbitals with parallel spins according to Hund's rule. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: For a spontaneous process, total entropy change of universe is positive. Explanation: Natural processes tend toward increased randomness.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Second law of thermodynamics states that entropy of universe increases in spontaneous processes because systems tend toward maximum disorder. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Heat capacity at constant pressure is greater than at constant volume. Explanation: At constant pressure, system does work, requiring additional heat.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Cp – Cv = R for ideal gas. At constant P, heat supplied does work of expansion, so more heat needed for same temperature rise. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Equilibrium constant is independent of initial concentrations. Explanation: K is defined by ratio of equilibrium concentrations, which is constant at given T.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
