options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Law of mass action states that at equilibrium, [products]/[reactants] ratio is constant regardless of starting amounts. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Standard electrode potential is an intensive property. Explanation: E° does not depend on the amount of substance.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Intensive properties are independent of quantity. E° is measured per mole of electrons and doesn't change with electrode size. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Molar conductivity increases with dilution. Explanation: Interionic attractions decrease on dilution, increasing ionic mobility.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: On dilution, ions are farther apart, reducing interionic attractions and increasing mobility, thus molar conductivity increases. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Acidic strength of oxyacids increases with oxidation state of central atom. Explanation: Higher oxidation state withdraws electrons more strongly, stabilizing conjugate base.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: Example: HClO (+1) weak, HClO₄ (+7) strongest. Higher oxidation state increases electronegativity of central atom, weakening O–H bond and stabilizing conjugate base. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Principle: Thermal stability of hydrides of group 15 decreases down the group. Explanation: Bond dissociation energy of E–H bond decreases as size increases.

options.A: Principle and explanation are correct and explanation justifies principle
options.B: Principle and explanation are correct but explanation does NOT justify principle
options.C: Principle is correct but explanation is incorrect
options.D: Principle is incorrect but explanation is correct

questions.explanation: As we move down group 15, atomic size increases, E–H bond length increases, bond strength decreases, so thermal stability decreases. Explanation correctly justifies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of the compound CH₃–CH–CH₂–CH–CH₃ | | Cl CH₃
questions.explanation: The longest carbon chain has 5 carbons (pentane). Numbering from the end nearest the substituent gives Cl at C-2 and CH₃ at C-4. IUPAC name: 2-chloro-4-methylpentane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will not form a yellow precipitate on heating with an alkaline solution of iodine?
questions.explanation: Iodoform test is positive for compounds containing CH₃CO– group or CH₃CH(OH)– group. Methanol (CH₃OH) does not have this structural feature, so it gives a negative iodoform test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The complex [Co(NH₃)₆]³⁺ is formed by sp³d² hybridisation. Hence, the ion should possess
questions.explanation: sp³d² hybridisation involves 6 orbitals forming 6 equivalent bonds directed towards the corners of a regular octahedron. Coordination number 6 with sp³d² hybridisation always gives octahedral geometry.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following vitamins is water-soluble?
questions.explanation: Vitamins are classified as fat-soluble (A, D, E, K) and water-soluble (B-complex and C). Vitamin-B belongs to the water-soluble category.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Green chemistry means such reactions which

options.B: reduce the use and production of hazardous chemicals
options.C: are related to the depletion of ozone layer

questions.explanation: Green chemistry involves designing chemical products and processes that reduce or eliminate the use and generation of hazardous substances, thus preventing pollution at the source.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following has the highest dipole moment?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen has greater electronegativity than P, As, and Sb. As we move down the group, electronegativity decreases, so dipole moment decreases. NH₃ has the highest dipole moment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Washing soda has formula
questions.explanation: Washing soda is chemically sodium carbonate decahydrate. Its formula is Na₂CO₃·10H₂O, containing 10 molecules of water of crystallization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following ions is the most stable in aqueous solution?
questions.explanation: Cr³⁺ has electronic configuration [Ar]3d³, which is half-filled in t₂g orbitals giving extra stability due to symmetrical distribution and exchange energy. Mn³⁺ (3d⁴) is less stable due to Jahn-Teller distortion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is most reactive towards electrophilic substitution?
questions.explanation: OCH₃ group has strong +M effect (electron-donating by resonance) which increases electron density on benzene ring, making it most reactive towards electrophilic substitution. NO₂ is electron-withdrawing and deactivates the ring.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction, 2Cl(g) → Cl₂(g) the correct option is
questions.explanation: Bond formation is exothermic (ΔH < 0). Two atoms combining into one molecule decreases randomness (ΔS < 0). Both enthalpy and entropy changes are negative.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of N-O bond lengths in NO, NO₂, NO₃⁻ and N₂O₄ is
questions.explanation: Bond order increases as: NO₃⁻ (1.33) < NO₂ (1.5) < N₂O₄ (1.5) < NO (2.5). Bond length is inversely proportional to bond order, so NO₃⁻ has longest bond, NO has shortest.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Standard electrode potentials are Fe²⁺/Fe: E° = –0.44 V, Fe³⁺/Fe²⁺: E° = 0.77 V. Fe²⁺, Fe³⁺ and Fe block are kept together, then
questions.explanation: Fe can reduce Fe³⁺ to Fe²⁺: Fe + 2Fe³⁺ → 3Fe²⁺. E°cell = E°cathode - E°anode = 0.77 - (-0.44) = 1.21 V (positive). The reaction is spontaneous, so Fe³⁺ decreases and Fe²⁺ increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the reaction, H–C≡CH → (i) NaNH₂/liq.NH₃ → (ii) CH₃CH₂Br → X → (i) NaNH₂/liq.NH₃ → (ii) CH₃CH₂Br → Y, X and Y are
questions.explanation: Acetylene → NaNH₂ forms acetylide → with CH₃CH₂Br gives 1-butyne (X). 1-butyne → NaNH₂ forms acetylide → with CH₃CH₂Br gives 3-hexyne (Y).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following is most acidic?
questions.explanation: Nitro group is electron-withdrawing (-I and -M effect), which stabilizes the phenoxide ion by delocalizing negative charge. Electron-donating groups (CH₃, OCH₃) decrease acidity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct increasing order of basic strength for the following compounds is I. NH₂ (aniline) II. NH₂–NO₂ (p-nitroaniline) III. NH₂–CH₃ (p-toluidine)
questions.explanation: NO₂ is electron-withdrawing, decreases basicity (least basic). CH₃ is electron-donating, increases basicity (most basic). Unsubstituted aniline has intermediate basicity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molecule which does not exhibit dipole moment is
questions.explanation: CCl₄ has symmetrical tetrahedral structure with all bond dipoles canceling each other, resulting in zero net dipole moment. NH₃, CHCl₃, and H₂O are asymmetrical with non-zero dipole moments.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reaction of benzaldehyde with ethanolic KCN gives
questions.explanation: Two molecules of benzaldehyde undergo benzoin condensation in presence of ethanolic KCN to form benzoin (α-hydroxyketone) - C₆H₅CH(OH)COC₆H₅.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following is not a condensation polymer?
questions.explanation: Neoprene is an addition polymer of chloroprene (2-chloro-1,3-butadiene). Melamine, glyptal, and dacron are condensation polymers formed with elimination of small molecules like water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following 0.10 M aqueous solutions will have the lowest freezing point?
questions.explanation: Freezing point depression depends on number of particles (van't Hoff factor i). Al₂(SO₄)₃ gives 5 ions (i = 5), KI gives 2 ions (i = 2), sugars give 1 particle (i = 1). Higher i gives greater depression.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following fluorides does not exist?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen does not have vacant d-orbitals in its valence shell, so it cannot expand its octet beyond 8 electrons. Therefore, NF₅ cannot exist, while P, As, Sb have d-orbitals and can form pentahalides.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The sum of coordination number and oxidation number of the metal M in the complex [M(en)₂(C₂O₄)]Cl is
questions.explanation: en is bidentate (2 × 2 = 4), C₂O₄²⁻ is bidentate (2). Total coordination number = 6. Let oxidation state of M be x: x + 0 + (-2) + (-1) = 0 → x = +3. Sum = 6 + 3 = 9. Correct answer should be 9, but given answer is 6 (likely error in question).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If ΔE is the heat of reaction for C₂H₅OH(l) + 3O₂(g) → 2CO₂(g) + 3H₂O(l) at constant volume, then ΔH (heat of reaction at constant pressure) is
questions.explanation: ΔH = ΔE + Δn_gRT. Δn_g = moles of gaseous products - moles of gaseous reactants = 2 - 3 = -1. Therefore, ΔH = ΔE + (-1)RT = ΔE – RT.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard emf of a galvanic cell involving cell reaction with n = 2 is found to be 0.295 V at 25°C. The equilibrium constant of the reaction would be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An alkene on ozonolysis gives methanol as one of the product. Its structure is
questions.explanation: 2-methylpropene on ozonolysis gives acetone and formaldehyde. Formaldehyde (methanal) is the product mentioned. Ozonolysis cleaves double bond to give carbonyl compounds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is not true regarding (+) lactose?

options.A: (+) lactose is a β-glycoside formed by D-(+)-glucose and D-(+)-galactose
options.B: (+) lactose is a reducing sugar and does not exhibit mutarotation
options.D: On hydrolysis, (+) lactose gives equal amounts of D-glucose and D-galactose

questions.explanation: Lactose is a reducing sugar and DOES exhibit mutarotation because the glucose unit has a free anomeric carbon. Statement (b) is incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of decreasing ionic radii among the following isoelectronic species is
questions.explanation: For isoelectronic species, ionic radius ∝ 1/Z_eff. Nuclear charge order: Ca²⁺ (20) > K⁺ (19) > Cl⁻ (17) > S²⁻ (16). Therefore, radius order: S²⁻ > Cl⁻ > K⁺ > Ca²⁺.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will not undergo S_N1 reaction with OH⁻?
questions.explanation: S_N1 reaction depends on carbocation stability. (CH₃)₃CCl forms stable 3° carbocation, benzyl chloride forms resonance-stabilized carbocation, allyl chloride forms stable allyl carbocation. n-Propyl chloride forms unstable 1° carbocation, so S_N1 is difficult.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Consider the following reactions, (i) CH₃CH₂CH₂Br + KOH → CH₃CH=CH₂ + KBr + H₂O (ii) (CH₃)₃CBr + KOH → (CH₃)₃COH + KBr (iii) CH₃CH₂Br + KOH → CH₃CH₂OH + KBr Which of the following statements is correct?

options.A: (i) is elimination, (ii) is substitution, (iii) is substitution
options.B: (i) is elimination, (ii) and (iii) are substitution reactions
options.C: (i) is substitution, (ii) and (iii) are addition reactions

questions.explanation: Reaction (i) forms alkene (elimination). Reaction (ii) with 3° alkyl halide gives alcohol (substitution). Reaction (iii) with 1° alkyl halide gives alcohol (substitution).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the E°cell for a given reaction has a negative value, which of the following gives correct relationships for the values of ΔG° and Keq?
questions.explanation: ΔG° = –nFE°cell. If E°cell is negative, ΔG° is positive. ΔG° = –RT ln Keq. If ΔG° is positive, ln Keq is negative, so Keq < 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Among the following ions, the pπ–dπ overlap could be present in
questions.explanation: pπ–dπ bonding occurs when the central atom has vacant d-orbitals. P has vacant 3d orbitals that can overlap with p-orbitals of oxygen. N and C do not have d-orbitals, so no pπ–dπ bonding.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a sample of perfect gas when its pressure is changed isothermally from p_i to p_f, the entropy change is given by
questions.explanation: For isothermal process, ΔS = nR ln(V_f/V_i). From Boyle's law, V_f/V_i = p_i/p_f. Therefore, ΔS = nR ln(p_i/p_f).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH₃–CH–CH₂–CH–CH₃ | | Br C₂H₅
questions.explanation: The longest chain has 6 carbons (hexane). Numbering from the end nearest the substituent gives Br at C-2 and CH₃ at C-4. Ethyl group is part of main chain as methyl branch.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Lucas reagent is
questions.explanation: Lucas reagent is an equimolar mixture of concentrated hydrochloric acid and anhydrous zinc chloride. It is used to distinguish between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The coordination number and oxidation state of Cr in K₃[Cr(C₂O₄)₃] are respectively
questions.explanation: C₂O₄²⁻ is bidentate ligand, so 3 ligands give coordination number 6. Oxidation state: 3(+1) + x + 3(-2) = 0 → x = +3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The human body does not produce
questions.explanation: Vitamins are organic compounds required in small amounts that cannot be synthesized by the human body and must be obtained from diet. DNA, hormones, and enzymes are produced by the body.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The gas that can readily decolourise acidified KMnO₄ solution is
questions.explanation: SO₂ acts as a reducing agent and reduces purple MnO₄⁻ to colourless Mn²⁺: 2MnO₄⁻ + 5SO₂ + 2H₂O → 2Mn²⁺ + 5SO₄²⁻ + 4H⁺.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following bonds will be most polar?
questions.explanation: Polarity depends on electronegativity difference. Electronegativity values: N = 3.0, F = 4.0, difference = 1.0. N–Cl (0.5), O–F (0.5), N–N (0). N–F has maximum difference, hence most polar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Bleaching powder is obtained by the action of chlorine gas and
questions.explanation: Bleaching powder (CaOCl₂) is prepared by passing chlorine gas over dry slaked lime [Ca(OH)₂]: Ca(OH)₂ + Cl₂ → CaOCl₂ + H₂O.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The compound that will react most readily with gaseous bromine has the formula
questions.explanation: Gaseous bromine undergoes electrophilic addition with alkenes readily. Ethene (C₂H₄) has a double bond and reacts immediately with bromine. Alkanes require light for substitution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following ions will exhibit colour in aqueous solutions?
questions.explanation: Colour in transition metal ions is due to d-d transitions requiring unpaired d-electrons. Ti³⁺ has d¹ configuration (one unpaired electron). Others have d⁰ or d¹⁰ configuration (no unpaired electrons).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The rate of a first-order reaction is 0.04 mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹ at 10 sec and 0.03 mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹ at 20 sec. The half-life period of the reaction is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the most stable carbocation?
questions.explanation: Benzyl carbocation is stabilized by resonance with the benzene ring (positive charge delocalized). (CH₃)₃C⁺ is stabilized by hyperconjugation (9 α-H). Benzyl is more stable than tertiary alkyl carbocation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which one of the following compounds will undergo self aldol condensation in the presence of cold dilute alkali?
questions.explanation: Aldol condensation requires α-hydrogen atoms. CH₃CH₂CHO has α-hydrogens. CH₂=CH–CHO and HC≡C–CHO have α-hydrogens but are less common. C₆H₅CHO has no α-hydrogen, so no aldol condensation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A 0.0020 m aqueous solution of an ionic compound [Co(NH₃)₅(NO₂)]Cl freezes at –0.00732°C. Number of moles of ions which 1 mole of ionic compound produces on being dissolved in water will be (kf = 1.86°C/m)
questions.explanation: ΔTf = i × kf × m. i = ΔTf/(kf × m) = 0.00732/(1.86 × 0.0020) = 0.00732/0.00372 = 1.97 ≈ 2. The compound gives 2 ions: [Co(NH₃)₅(NO₂)]⁺ and Cl⁻.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The enthalpy of fusion of water is 1.435 kcal/mol. The molar entropy change for the melting of ice at 0°C is
questions.explanation: ΔS = ΔH_fusion/T = 1.435 kcal/mol / 273 K = 1435 cal/mol / 273 K = 5.26 cal/mol·K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a nitric acid anhydride?
questions.explanation: N₂O₅ (dinitrogen pentoxide) is the anhydride of HNO₃: N₂O₅ + H₂O → 2HNO₃. NO₂ is mixed anhydride, N₂O₃ is anhydride of HNO₂.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In a typical fuel cell, the reactants (R) and product (P) are

options.C: R = H₂(g), O₂(g), Cl₂(g); P = HClO₄(aq)

questions.explanation: In a typical hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell, hydrogen and oxygen react to form water with release of electrical energy: 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following equilibrium Kc and Kp are not equal?
questions.explanation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. Δn = n_p(g) - n_r(g) = 2 - 1 = 1. So Kp ≠ Kc. For others, Δn = 0, so Kp = Kc.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Acetylenic hydrogens are acidic because

options.A: sigma electron density of C–H bond in acetylene is nearer to carbon which has 50% s-character
options.B: acetylene has only one hydrogen on each carbon
options.C: acetylene contains least number of hydrogens
options.D: acetylene belongs to alkynes with formula CₙH₂ₙ₋₂

questions.explanation: sp-hybridized carbon has 50% s-character, making it more electronegative. The C–H bond electrons are pulled toward carbon, making hydrogen slightly positive and acidic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When phenol is heated with CHCl₃ and alcoholic KOH, salicylaldehyde is produced. This reaction is known as
questions.explanation: Reimer-Tiemann reaction involves electrophilic substitution of phenol with chloroform in presence of alkali to introduce –CHO group at ortho position, forming salicylaldehyde.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Acetamide is treated with the following reagents separately. Which one would yield methyl amine?
questions.explanation: Hofmann bromamide degradation: CH₃CONH₂ + Br₂ + 4NaOH → CH₃NH₂ + 2NaBr + Na₂CO₃ + 2H₂O. This converts amide to amine with one carbon less.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The common oxidation states of Ti are
questions.explanation: Titanium (atomic number 22) with electronic configuration [Ar]3d²4s² commonly exhibits +3 and +4 oxidation states. +4 is most stable (Ti⁴⁺ has noble gas configuration).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Formalin is an aqueous solution of
questions.explanation: Formalin is a 40% aqueous solution of formaldehyde (HCHO) with 8% methanol as stabilizer. It is used as a disinfectant and for preserving biological specimens.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Nylon-66 is a polyamide obtained by the reaction of
questions.explanation: Nylon-66 is made from adipic acid [HOOC-(CH₂)₄-COOH] and hexamethylenediamine [H₂N-(CH₂)₆-NH₂] by condensation polymerization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following salts will give highest pH in water?
questions.explanation: Na₂CO₃ is a salt of strong base (NaOH) and weak acid (H₂CO₃). On hydrolysis, it produces OH⁻ ions, giving basic solution with pH > 7. KCl and NaCl are neutral, CuSO₄ is acidic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is most acidic?
questions.explanation: Acidic nature of oxides decreases down the group. N is most electronegative, so N₂O₅ is most acidic. As electronegativity decreases, acidic character decreases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of increasing field strength of ligands to form coordination compounds is
questions.explanation: Spectrochemical series orders ligands by increasing field strength: I⁻ < Br⁻ < SCN⁻ < Cl⁻ < F⁻ < OH⁻ < C₂O₄²⁻ < H₂O < NCS⁻ < NH₃ < en < CN⁻ < CO.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The absolute enthalpy of neutralisation of the reaction HCl + NaOH → NaCl + H₂O is
questions.explanation: Enthalpy of neutralization of a strong acid with strong base is constant: –57.1 kJ/mol (–13.7 kcal/mol). It's the heat released when 1 mole of H⁺ and 1 mole of OH⁻ form 1 mole of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potentials for Zn²⁺/Zn, Fe²⁺/Fe and Ni²⁺/Ni are –0.76 V, –0.44 V and –0.25 V respectively. The correct order of reducing power of metals is
questions.explanation: Reducing power ∝ 1/E°red. More negative E°red means stronger reducing agent. Order: Zn (–0.76) > Fe (–0.44) > Ni (–0.25).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The reaction of toluene with Cl₂ in the presence of FeCl₃ gives X and reaction in presence of light gives Y. X and Y are

options.C: X = o and p-chlorotoluene, Y = benzyl chloride

questions.explanation: FeCl₃ promotes electrophilic substitution on ring (o/p-chlorotoluene). Light promotes free radical substitution on side chain (benzyl chloride).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Enzymes are made up of
questions.explanation: Enzymes are globular proteins with specific three-dimensional structures that act as biological catalysts. They have active sites that bind substrates specifically.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a first-order reaction, the half-life period is independent of
questions.explanation: For first order reaction, t₁/₂ = 0.693/k, which is constant and independent of initial concentration. For other orders, t₁/₂ depends on initial concentration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of decreasing acidic strength of trichloroacetic acid (A), trifluoroacetic acid (B), acetic acid (C) and formic acid (D) is
questions.explanation: Electron-withdrawing groups increase acidity. CF₃ has stronger –I effect than CCl₃. Formic acid (D) is stronger than acetic acid (C) due to less +I effect of H vs CH₃. Order: CF₃COOH > CCl₃COOH > HCOOH > CH₃COOH.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The complex [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]⁺ has how many geometrical isomers?
questions.explanation: [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]⁺ is an octahedral complex of type [MA₄B₂]. It exhibits geometrical isomerism with cis and trans isomers (two geometrical isomers).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction, PCl₅(g) ⇌ PCl₃(g) + Cl₂(g), which of the following conditions are correct?
questions.explanation: Bond breaking is endothermic (ΔH > 0). One molecule dissociates into two molecules, increasing randomness (ΔS > 0).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Four successive members of the first row transition elements are Vanadium (23), Chromium (24), Manganese (25) and Iron (26). Which one has the highest third ionisation enthalpy?
questions.explanation: Mn has electronic configuration [Ar]3d⁵4s². Mn²⁺ has stable half-filled d⁵ configuration. Removing third electron from Mn²⁺ (d⁵) requires high energy due to stability of half-filled shell.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The compound formed when malonic acid is heated with urea is
questions.explanation: Malonic acid (HOOC–CH₂–COOH) condenses with urea (H₂N–CO–NH₂) to form barbituric acid, a cyclic compound used in synthesis of barbiturates (sedatives).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The cell reaction for the given cell Pt|H₂(1 atm)|HCl(0.1 M)||CH₃COOH(0.1 M)|H₂(1 atm)|Pt will have EMF not zero because

options.A: EMF depends on molarities of acids used
options.B: pH of 0.1 M HCl and 0.1 M CH₃COOH is not same
options.D: acids used in two compartments are different

questions.explanation: HCl is strong acid, completely dissociated giving [H⁺] = 0.1 M (pH = 1). CH₃COOH is weak acid, partially dissociated giving lower [H⁺] (pH ≈ 2.87). Different [H⁺] produces potential difference.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: White phosphorus is
questions.explanation: White phosphorus exists as P₄ molecules with tetrahedral structure. Each P atom is bonded to three other P atoms. It is highly reactive and stored under water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Standard enthalpy and standard entropy changes for the oxidation of ammonia at 298 K are –382.64 kJ mol⁻¹ and –145.6 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹ respectively. Standard Gibbs energy change for the same reaction at 298 K is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Benzene reacts with n-propyl chloride in the presence of anhydrous AlCl₃ to give
questions.explanation: In Friedel-Crafts alkylation with n-propyl chloride, the intermediate n-propyl carbocation rearranges to more stable isopropyl carbocation, giving isopropylbenzene (cumene) as product.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The monomer of the polymer ⎡ ⎤ ⎢ –CH₂–C–CH₂–CH– ⎥ ⎢ | | ⎥ ⎢ CH₃ CH₃ ⎥ ⎣ ⎦n is
questions.explanation: The polymer shown is polyisoprene (natural rubber). The monomer is isoprene: CH₂=C(CH₃)–CH=CH₂ (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene). It undergoes 1,4-addition polymerization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of the compound CH₃–C≡C–CH=CH₂ is
questions.explanation: Double bond gets priority over triple bond in numbering. Numbering from the end nearest the double bond gives double bond at C-1 and triple bond at C-3. Name: pent-1-en-3-yne.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The compound which reacts fastest with Lucas reagent at room temperature is
questions.explanation: Lucas reagent (HCl + ZnCl₂) reacts with alcohols to form alkyl chlorides. Tertiary alcohols react immediately (turbidity appears instantly). 2-methylpropan-2-ol is a tertiary alcohol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of [Pt(NH₃)₃(Br)(NO₂)Cl]Cl is
questions.explanation: Ligands are named alphabetically: ammine, bromo, chloro, nitro. Oxidation state of Pt: NH₃(0), Br(–1), NO₂(–1), Cl(–1), outer Cl(–1): x + 0 –1 –1 –1 –1 = 0 → x = +4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The substance which is mainly responsible for the greenhouse effect is
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide is the primary greenhouse gas responsible for global warming. It absorbs infrared radiation and traps heat in the atmosphere.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The bond angle in water molecule is approximately
questions.explanation: Water has sp³ hybridization with two lone pairs and two bond pairs. Lone pair-lone pair repulsion reduces bond angle from ideal 109.5° to 104.5°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following metals is extracted by the electrolysis of its molten chloride?
questions.explanation: Sodium is extracted by electrolysis of molten NaCl (Down's process). Aluminum is extracted from Al₂O₃ (not chloride). Fe and Cu are extracted by reduction methods.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following alkenes will give acetone on ozonolysis?
questions.explanation: 2-methyl-2-butene on ozonolysis gives acetone (CH₃–CO–CH₃) and acetaldehyde (CH₃–CHO). Acetone is one of the products.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of unpaired electrons in Mn²⁺ ion is
questions.explanation: Mn (Z=25) electronic configuration: [Ar]3d⁵4s². Mn²⁺ loses 2 electrons from 4s, giving [Ar]3d⁵. d⁵ configuration has 5 unpaired electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The unit of rate constant for a first order reaction is
questions.explanation: For first order reaction, rate = k[A]. So k = rate/[A] = (mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹)/(mol L⁻¹) = s⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The most stable carbocation among the following is
questions.explanation: Tertiary carbocation (CH₃)₃C⁺ is stabilized by hyperconjugation from 9 α C–H bonds. Allyl carbocation is also stable but tertiary carbocation is more stable under normal conditions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will give iodoform test?
questions.explanation: Iodoform test is positive for compounds with CH₃–CO– group or CH₃–CH(OH)– group. Acetophenone (C₆H₅–CO–CH₃) has methyl ketone group and gives positive iodoform test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The magnetic moment of [NiCl₄]²⁻ is
questions.explanation: In [NiCl₄]²⁻, Ni²⁺ (d⁸) has sp³ hybridization (tetrahedral) due to weak field Cl⁻ ligands. It has 2 unpaired electrons. μ = √[n(n+2)] = √[2×4] = √8 = 2.83 BM.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The osmotic pressure of a solution is given by the relation
questions.explanation: For dilute solutions, osmotic pressure π = CRT, where C is molar concentration (mol/L), R is gas constant, T is temperature. This is van't Hoff equation for osmotic pressure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potential for Cu²⁺/Cu is +0.34 V and for Ag⁺/Ag is +0.80 V. The cell potential for the cell Cu|Cu²⁺(1 M)||Ag⁺(1 M)|Ag is
questions.explanation: E°cell = E°cathode – E°anode = E°Ag⁺/Ag – E°Cu²⁺/Cu = 0.80 – 0.34 = 0.46 V. The reaction is: Cu + 2Ag⁺ → Cu²⁺ + 2Ag.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For which of the following reactions, Kp = Kc?
questions.explanation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. For H₂ + I₂ ⇌ 2HI, Δn = 2 – 2 = 0, so Kp = Kc. For others, Δn ≠ 0, so Kp ≠ Kc.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed when acetylene is passed through dilute H₂SO₄ in presence of HgSO₄ is
questions.explanation: Hydration of acetylene in presence of HgSO₄ and dilute H₂SO₄ gives acetaldehyde via vinyl alcohol intermediate (tautomerization): HC≡CH + H₂O → [CH₂=CH–OH] → CH₃–CHO.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following amines will give carbylamine test?
questions.explanation: Carbylamine test is given only by primary amines (aliphatic or aromatic). Aniline (C₆H₅–NH₂) is a primary aromatic amine and gives positive test. Secondary and tertiary amines do not respond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following species has unpaired electrons in its ground state?
questions.explanation: Molecular orbital configuration of O₂ shows two unpaired electrons in π* orbitals, making it paramagnetic. N₂, CO, and CN⁻ have all electrons paired (diamagnetic).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following ions is coloured in aqueous solution?
questions.explanation: Cu²⁺ has d⁹ configuration with one unpaired electron, showing d-d transitions and colour. Sc³⁺ (d⁰), Ti⁴⁺ (d⁰), and Zn²⁺ (d¹⁰) have no unpaired electrons and are colourless.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Benzaldehyde reacts with alcoholic KCN to give
questions.explanation: Benzaldehyde undergoes benzoin condensation in presence of alcoholic KCN to form benzoin (C₆H₅–CH(OH)–CO–C₆H₅). KCN acts as catalyst for this condensation reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a biodegradable polymer?
questions.explanation: Nylon-2-nylon-6 is an alternating polyamide of glycine and amino caproic acid. It is biodegradable because of amide linkages that can be broken down by enzymes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction A → B, the rate constant is 0.693 × 10⁻³ s⁻¹. The time required for 50% completion of the reaction is
questions.explanation: For first order reaction, t₁/₂ = 0.693/k = 0.693/(0.693 × 10⁻³) = 1/10⁻³ = 1000 s.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The van't Hoff factor for a dilute aqueous solution of BaCl₂ is
questions.explanation: BaCl₂ dissociates completely in aqueous solution: BaCl₂ → Ba²⁺ + 2Cl⁻. Total number of ions = 3, so van't Hoff factor i = 3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following elements does not show allotropy?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen exists as diatomic molecule N₂ and does not show allotropy. Carbon (diamond, graphite), phosphorus (white, red, black), and sulphur (rhombic, monoclinic) show allotropy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following ligands is bidentate?
questions.explanation: en (ethylenediamine, H₂N–CH₂–CH₂–NH₂) has two donor nitrogen atoms and is bidentate. NH₃, H₂O, and CO are monodentate ligands.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For an ideal gas, Cp – Cv is equal to
questions.explanation: For one mole of ideal gas, Cp – Cv = R, where R is universal gas constant. This is Mayer's relation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of Faradays required to produce 20 g of calcium from molten CaCl₂ is
questions.explanation: Ca²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Ca. 1 mole Ca (40 g) requires 2 F. For 20 g (0.5 mole), charge required = 0.5 × 2 = 1 F.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the shortest C–C bond length?
questions.explanation: Bond length order: triple bond < double bond < single bond. Ethyne has C≡C triple bond (1.20 Å), ethene C=C (1.34 Å), ethane C–C (1.54 Å), benzene intermediate (1.39 Å).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The linkage between two amino acids in a protein is called
questions.explanation: Amino acids are linked by peptide bonds (–CO–NH–) formed between carboxyl group of one amino acid and amino group of another with elimination of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The shape of I₃⁻ ion is
questions.explanation: I₃⁻ has sp³d hybridization with three lone pairs and two bond pairs, giving linear geometry. The ion is symmetrical: I–I–I with bond angle 180°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The rate constant of a reaction is 1.5 × 10⁻² min⁻¹. The order of the reaction is
questions.explanation: Unit of rate constant for first order reaction is time⁻¹ (min⁻¹, s⁻¹). For zero order: mol L⁻¹ time⁻¹, second order: L mol⁻¹ time⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: Geometrical isomerism requires: (i) double bond, (ii) each doubly bonded carbon has two different groups. But-2-ene (CH₃–CH=CH–CH₃) satisfies this condition.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The equilibrium constant for the reaction N₂(g) + O₂(g) ⇌ 2NO(g) is K. The equilibrium constant for the reaction NO(g) ⇌ ½N₂(g) + ½O₂(g) is
questions.explanation: For N₂ + O₂ ⇌ 2NO, K = [NO]²/[N₂][O₂]. For reverse reaction 2NO ⇌ N₂ + O₂, K' = 1/K. For half reaction NO ⇌ ½N₂ + ½O₂, K'' = √(1/K) = 1/√K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Lanthanoid contraction is responsible for the fact that

options.A: Zr and Y have about the same radius
options.B: Zr and Nb have similar oxidation state
options.C: Zr and Hf have about the same radius
options.D: Zr and Zn have the same oxidation state

questions.explanation: Due to lanthanoid contraction, elements of 4d and 5d series have similar atomic/ionic radii. Zr (40) and Hf (72) have almost identical radii (160 pm and 158 pm).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The reagent used to distinguish between acetaldehyde and acetone is
questions.explanation: Both acetaldehyde and acetone give iodoform test (positive with I₂+NaOH) because both have CH₃–CO– group. This question needs correction - both give positive test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potentials for Zn²⁺/Zn, Cu²⁺/Cu and Ag⁺/Ag are –0.76 V, +0.34 V and +0.80 V respectively. The correct order of decreasing reducing power is
questions.explanation: Reducing power ∝ 1/E°red. More negative E°red means stronger reducing agent. Order: Zn (–0.76) > Cu (+0.34) > Ag (+0.80).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds does not exist?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen does not have d-orbitals to expand its octet beyond 8 electrons. It cannot form NF₅. Phosphorus, arsenic, and antimony have d-orbitals and can form pentafluorides.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The Gibbs free energy change ΔG is related to equilibrium constant K by the equation
questions.explanation: Standard Gibbs free energy change is related to equilibrium constant by ΔG° = –RT ln K. At equilibrium, ΔG = 0 and Q = K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed in the reaction of 1,3-butadiene with HBr at low temperature is
questions.explanation: At low temperature, 1,3-butadiene undergoes 1,2-addition giving 3-bromo-1-butene. At higher temperature, 1,4-addition predominates giving 1-bromo-2-butene.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Terylene is a condensation polymer of
questions.explanation: Terylene (Dacron) is a polyester formed by condensation polymerization of ethylene glycol (HO–CH₂–CH₂–OH) and terephthalic acid (HOOC–C₆H₄–COOH).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH₃–CH–CH₂–CHO is | CH₃
questions.explanation: The longest chain has 4 carbons with aldehyde group (butanal). Numbering starts from aldehyde carbon as C-1. Methyl group at C-3 gives name 3-methylbutanal.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following alcohols will give a positive iodoform test?
questions.explanation: Iodoform test is positive for compounds with CH₃–CH(OH)– group or CH₃–CO– group. Ethanol (CH₃CH₂OH) has CH₃–CH(OH)– structure and gives positive test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The coordination number of Ni in [Ni(C₂O₄)₃]⁴⁻ is
questions.explanation: C₂O₄²⁻ (oxalate) is a bidentate ligand. Three oxalate ligands give 3 × 2 = 6 coordination number. Ni is coordinated through six oxygen atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which vitamin is known as ascorbic acid?
questions.explanation: Vitamin C is chemically known as ascorbic acid. It is water-soluble and acts as an antioxidant. Deficiency causes scurvy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Photochemical smog is formed in
questions.explanation: Photochemical smog is formed by action of sunlight on primary pollutants (NOx and hydrocarbons) in warm, sunny climates. It contains ozone, PAN, and aldehydes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has maximum bond angle?
questions.explanation: CO₂ has linear structure with bond angle 180°. CH₄ (109.5°), NH₃ (107.5°), H₂O (104.5°). CO₂ has maximum bond angle due to sp hybridization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Sodium carbonate is commonly known as
questions.explanation: Na₂CO₃·10H₂O is washing soda. Baking soda is NaHCO₃, caustic soda is NaOH, soda ash is anhydrous Na₂CO₃.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of sigma bonds in ethane molecule is
questions.explanation: Ethane (CH₃–CH₃) has 6 C–H sigma bonds and 1 C–C sigma bond, total 7 sigma bonds. No pi bonds present.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not a transition element?
questions.explanation: Transition elements have partially filled d-orbitals. Zn has electronic configuration [Ar]3d¹⁰4s² (fully filled d-orbital), so it is not considered a transition element.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a zero order reaction, the plot of concentration vs time is
questions.explanation: For zero order reaction, [A] = [A]₀ – kt. Plot of [A] vs t gives straight line with slope = –k (negative slope).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct order of stability of the following carbocations is I. CH₃–CH₂⁺ II. (CH₃)₂CH⁺ III. (CH₃)₃C⁺ IV. CH₂=CH–CH₂⁺
questions.explanation: Allyl carbocation (IV) is stabilized by resonance. Tertiary carbocation (III) by hyperconjugation (9 α-H). Secondary (II) by 6 α-H. Primary (I) by 3 α-H. Order: allyl > 3° > 2° > 1°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Acetaldehyde reacts with hydroxylamine to form
questions.explanation: Aldehydes react with hydroxylamine (NH₂OH) to form oximes. CH₃–CHO + NH₂OH → CH₃–CH=N–OH + H₂O. Product is acetaldehyde oxime.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The freezing point depression constant for water is 1.86 K kg mol⁻¹. The freezing point of 0.1 m NaCl solution will be
questions.explanation: ΔTf = i × Kf × m. For NaCl, i = 2 (Na⁺ + Cl⁻). ΔTf = 2 × 1.86 × 0.1 = 0.372°C. Freezing point = 0 – 0.372 = –0.372°C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For which of the following processes will ΔS be negative?
questions.explanation: Entropy decreases when number of particles decreases. 2H atoms combine to form one H₂ molecule, randomness decreases (ΔS < 0). Other processes increase randomness.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is used in the preparation of chlorine?
questions.explanation: Chlorine is prepared by reaction of HCl with oxidizing agents: MnO₂ + 4HCl → MnCl₂ + Cl₂ + 2H₂O and 2KMnO₄ + 16HCl → 2KCl + 2MnCl₂ + 5Cl₂ + 8H₂O.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potential for Fe²⁺/Fe is –0.44 V and for Fe³⁺/Fe²⁺ is +0.77 V. The standard electrode potential for Fe³⁺/Fe is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In which of the following reactions, increase in pressure will favour forward reaction?
questions.explanation: Increase in pressure favours reaction with decrease in number of moles. For ammonia synthesis, Δn = 2 – 4 = –2 (decrease), so forward reaction favoured by high pressure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will give white precipitate with ammoniacal AgNO₃?
questions.explanation: Terminal alkynes (with ≡C–H) react with ammoniacal AgNO₃ to form white precipitate of silver acetylide. CH₃–C≡CH is a terminal alkyne, gives white precipitate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When phenol is treated with bromine water, the product formed is
questions.explanation: Phenol reacts with bromine water (polar medium) to give 2,4,6-tribromophenol as white precipitate. All ortho and para positions are substituted due to strong activating effect of –OH group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The reagent used to distinguish between primary, secondary and tertiary amines is
questions.explanation: Hinsberg's reagent (benzenesulphonyl chloride, C₆H₅SO₂Cl) reacts differently with 1°, 2°, and 3° amines, forming products with different solubility in alkali, thus distinguishing them.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules is paramagnetic?
questions.explanation: Molecular orbital configuration of O₂ shows two unpaired electrons in π* orbitals, making it paramagnetic. N₂, F₂, and Cl₂ have all electrons paired (diamagnetic).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The magnetic moment of Fe³⁺ ion is
questions.explanation: Fe³⁺ has d⁵ configuration with 5 unpaired electrons. μ = √[n(n+2)] = √[5×7] = √35 = 5.92 BM.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed in the reaction of acetone with iodine in presence of NaOH is
questions.explanation: Acetone (CH₃–CO–CH₃) undergoes iodoform reaction with I₂/NaOH to give yellow precipitate of iodoform (CHI₃) and sodium acetate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is an addition polymer?
questions.explanation: Polythene is formed by addition polymerization of ethene without elimination of any small molecule. Nylon, terylene, and bakelite are condensation polymers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The half-life of a first order reaction is 69.3 s. The rate constant of the reaction is
questions.explanation: For first order reaction, k = 0.693/t₁/₂ = 0.693/69.3 = 0.01 s⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following aqueous solutions has the highest boiling point?
questions.explanation: Boiling point elevation ∝ i × m. AlCl₃ → Al³⁺ + 3Cl⁻ (i = 4), CaCl₂ → Ca²⁺ + 2Cl⁻ (i = 3), NaCl (i = 2), glucose (i = 1). Highest i gives highest boiling point.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not an allotropic form of carbon?
questions.explanation: Silicon carbide (SiC) is a compound of carbon and silicon, not an allotropic form of pure carbon. Diamond, graphite, and fullerene are allotropes of carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a spontaneous reaction, the sign of ΔG, ΔH and ΔS at all temperatures should be
questions.explanation: For spontaneity at all temperatures, ΔG must be negative. When ΔH is negative (exothermic) and ΔS is positive (increase in randomness), ΔG is negative at all temperatures.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The equivalent conductance of NaCl at infinite dilution is 126.45 S cm² mol⁻¹. The equivalent conductance of Na⁺ ion is 50.1 S cm² mol⁻¹. The equivalent conductance of Cl⁻ ion is
questions.explanation: According to Kohlrausch's law, Λ°(NaCl) = λ°(Na⁺) + λ°(Cl⁻). So λ°(Cl⁻) = Λ°(NaCl) – λ°(Na⁺) = 126.45 – 50.1 = 76.35 S cm² mol⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds will not show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: For geometrical isomerism, each doubly bonded carbon must have two different groups. In CH₃–CH=CCl₂, one carbon has two Cl atoms (same group), so no geometrical isomerism.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a disaccharide?
questions.explanation: Sucrose is a disaccharide composed of glucose and fructose. Glucose and fructose are monosaccharides. Starch is a polysaccharide.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The hybridization of central atom in XeF₄ is
questions.explanation: XeF₄ has 4 bond pairs and 2 lone pairs on Xe. It undergoes sp³d² hybridization giving square planar geometry (two lone pairs occupy axial positions).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The activation energy of a reaction can be determined from the slope of the plot of
questions.explanation: Arrhenius equation: ln k = ln A – (Ea/R)(1/T). Plot of ln k vs 1/T gives straight line with slope = –Ea/R, from which activation energy Ea can be calculated.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will not show tautomerism?
questions.explanation: Tautomerism requires α-hydrogen atoms. Acetone and acetylacetone have α-hydrogens. Nitromethane shows nitro-aci tautomerism. Benzaldehyde has no α-hydrogen, so no tautomerism.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction 2HI(g) ⇌ H₂(g) + I₂(g), the degree of dissociation is α. The total number of moles at equilibrium starting with 2 moles of HI is
questions.explanation: 2HI ⇌ H₂ + I₂. Initial: 2 moles HI, 0, 0. At equilibrium: 2–2α moles HI, α moles H₂, α moles I₂. Total moles = (2–2α) + α + α = 2. Total moles remain constant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed in the reaction of benzaldehyde with 50% NaOH is

options.C: both benzoic acid and benzyl alcohol

questions.explanation: Benzaldehyde (no α-hydrogen) undergoes Cannizzaro reaction with concentrated NaOH, giving benzyl alcohol (reduction product) and sodium benzoate (oxidation product).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The emf of the cell Zn|Zn²⁺(0.01 M)||Cu²⁺(1.0 M)|Cu at 25°C is (E°cell = 1.10 V)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about ozone is correct?

options.C: Ozone is an allotrope of oxygen
options.D: Ozone is stable at room temperature

questions.explanation: Ozone (O₃) is an allotrope of oxygen with bent structure (bond angle 117°). It is diamagnetic and unstable at room temperature, decomposing to oxygen.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction, 2SO₂(g) + O₂(g) ⇌ 2SO₃(g), ΔH = –198 kJ. The value of ΔH for the reaction SO₃(g) ⇌ SO₂(g) + ½O₂(g) is
questions.explanation: For reverse reaction: 2SO₃ ⇌ 2SO₂ + O₂, ΔH = +198 kJ. For half reaction: SO₃ ⇌ SO₂ + ½O₂, ΔH = +198/2 = +99 kJ.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the free radical chlorination of methane, the chain initiating step involves
questions.explanation: Chain initiation step involves homolytic fission of Cl–Cl bond by UV light: Cl₂ → 2Cl•. These chlorine free radicals then attack methane in propagation steps.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The monomer of natural rubber is
questions.explanation: Natural rubber is polyisoprene, polymer of isoprene (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene). It has cis configuration at double bonds, giving elastic properties.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH₃–CH–CH₂–CH₂–Br is | C₂H₅
questions.explanation: The longest chain has 5 carbons (pentane). Numbering from the end nearest the functional group (Br) gives Br at C-1. The ethyl group becomes part of main chain as methyl at C-3. Name: 1-bromo-3-methylpentane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a tertiary alcohol?
questions.explanation: Tertiary alcohol has –OH group attached to a carbon that is bonded to three other carbon atoms. (CH₃)₃C–OH (tert-butyl alcohol) satisfies this condition.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which vitamin is necessary for blood clotting?
questions.explanation: Vitamin K is essential for synthesis of prothrombin and other blood clotting factors in liver. Deficiency causes increased bleeding time.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The major component of acid rain is
questions.explanation: Acid rain contains mainly H₂SO₄ and HNO₃ formed from SO₂ and NOx emissions. These oxides dissolve in rainwater to form acids, lowering pH below 5.6.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest electronegativity?
questions.explanation: Electronegativity decreases down the group. Fluorine has highest electronegativity (4.0 on Pauling scale) among all elements.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is aromatic?
questions.explanation: Benzene is aromatic as it follows Hückel's rule (4n+2 π electrons, n=1) with cyclic delocalization of 6 π electrons. Others are not aromatic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The common oxidation state of lanthanides is
questions.explanation: Lanthanides predominantly exhibit +3 oxidation state. Some show +2 and +4 oxidation states, but +3 is most common and stable for all lanthanides.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The rate constant for a first order reaction is 0.0693 min⁻¹. The half-life of the reaction is
questions.explanation: For first order reaction, t₁/₂ = 0.693/k = 0.693/0.0693 = 10 min.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest acid?
questions.explanation: Electron-withdrawing Cl atoms increase acidity by –I effect. More Cl atoms mean stronger –I effect, more stabilization of conjugate base. Trichloroacetic acid is strongest among given.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Acetone reacts with HCN to form
questions.explanation: Carbonyl compounds undergo nucleophilic addition with HCN to form cyanohydrins. Acetone + HCN → (CH₃)₂C(OH)CN (acetone cyanohydrin).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molality of a solution containing 20 g of NaOH in 500 g of water is
questions.explanation: Moles of NaOH = 20/40 = 0.5 mol. Mass of solvent = 500 g = 0.5 kg. Molality = moles of solute/kg of solvent = 0.5/0.5 = 1.0 m.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is used as a drying agent for gases?
questions.explanation: Phosphorus pentoxide (P₂O₅) is a powerful dehydrating agent and drying agent because it has strong affinity for water, forming phosphoric acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potential for Ag⁺/Ag is +0.80 V and for Cu²⁺/Cu is +0.34 V. The standard emf for the cell Cu|Cu²⁺||Ag⁺|Ag is
questions.explanation: E°cell = E°cathode – E°anode = E°Ag⁺/Ag – E°Cu²⁺/Cu = 0.80 – 0.34 = 0.46 V. The cell reaction is Cu + 2Ag⁺ → Cu²⁺ + 2Ag.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The equilibrium constant for the reaction 2SO₂ + O₂ ⇌ 2SO₃ is K. The equilibrium constant for the reaction SO₃ ⇌ SO₂ + ½O₂ is
questions.explanation: For reverse reaction 2SO₃ ⇌ 2SO₂ + O₂, K' = 1/K. For half reaction SO₃ ⇌ SO₂ + ½O₂, K'' = √(1/K) = 1/√K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will decolourise bromine water?
questions.explanation: Alkenes (C₂H₄) decolourise bromine water due to addition reaction across double bond. Alkanes do not decolourise bromine water in dark (require light for substitution).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When ethanol is heated with copper at 573 K, the product formed is
questions.explanation: Primary alcohols undergo dehydrogenation when passed over heated copper at 573 K to form aldehydes. Ethanol → acetaldehyde + H₂.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a secondary amine?
questions.explanation: Secondary amines have two alkyl/aryl groups attached to nitrogen. (CH₃)₂NH is dimethylamine (secondary). CH₃NH₂ (primary), (CH₃)₃N (tertiary), C₆H₅NH₂ (primary aromatic).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules has a linear structure?
questions.explanation: CO₂ has sp hybridization with two bond pairs and no lone pairs, giving linear geometry (O=C=O, 180°). H₂O (bent), NH₃ (pyramidal), SO₂ (bent) are non-linear.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The reagent used to distinguish between formaldehyde and acetaldehyde is
questions.explanation: Both give Tollen's and Fehling's tests positive. Iodoform test distinguishes: acetaldehyde (CH₃–CHO) gives positive iodoform test, formaldehyde (H–CHO) gives negative test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a thermosetting polymer?
questions.explanation: Bakelite is a thermosetting polymer - it undergoes irreversible cross-linking on heating and cannot be remoulded. Polythene, PVC, and polystyrene are thermoplastics (can be remoulded).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction, the rate constant at 500 K is twice that at 400 K. The activation energy of the reaction is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The van't Hoff factor for 0.1 M K₄[Fe(CN)₆] solution is
questions.explanation: K₄[Fe(CN)₆] dissociates as: K₄[Fe(CN)₆] → 4K⁺ + [Fe(CN)₆]⁴⁻. Total ions = 5, so van't Hoff factor i = 5.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: O₂ is not a greenhouse gas. Greenhouse gases (CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, CFCs, O₃, water vapor) absorb and emit infrared radiation, trapping heat in atmosphere.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The magnetic moment of [Fe(CN)₆]⁴⁻ is
questions.explanation: In [Fe(CN)₆]⁴⁻, Fe²⁺ (d⁶) is in low-spin configuration due to strong field CN⁻ ligands. All electrons paired, t₂g⁶ eg⁰. Magnetic moment = 0 BM (diamagnetic).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction to be spontaneous at all temperatures, the conditions are
questions.explanation: ΔG = ΔH – TΔS. For spontaneity at all temperatures, ΔG must be negative always. This requires ΔH < 0 (exothermic) and ΔS > 0 (increase in entropy).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds will undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction?
questions.explanation: Friedel-Crafts reaction requires electron-rich aromatic ring. Toluene (with electron-donating CH₃) undergoes reaction. Nitrobenzene and aniline (in acidic medium) do not react due to deactivation or salt formation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The prosthetic group in haemoglobin is
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin consists of globin protein and haem prosthetic group. Haem is an iron-porphyrin complex that binds oxygen. Prosthetic group is tightly bound non-protein part.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The bond order in O₂ molecule is
questions.explanation: Molecular orbital configuration of O₂: KK (σ2s)²(σ2s)²(σ2pz)²(π2px)²(π2py)²(π2px)¹(π*2py)¹. Bond order = (10 – 6)/2 = 2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The activation energy of a reaction is zero. The rate constant of the reaction
questions.explanation: Arrhenius equation: k = A e⁻ᴱᵃ/ᴿᵀ. If Ea = 0, then k = A, constant. Rate constant becomes independent of temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds will show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: For geometrical isomerism, each doubly bonded carbon must have two different groups. But-2-ene satisfies this condition. CH₃–CH=C(CH₃)₂ has one carbon with two CH₃ groups, so no geometrical isomerism.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The complex [Co(NH₃)₅(NO₂)]Cl₂ exists as two isomers. This is due to
questions.explanation: NO₂⁻ is an ambidentate ligand, can bond through N or O. [Co(NH₃)₅(NO₂)]²⁺ (nitro) and [Co(NH₃)₅(ONO)]²⁺ (nitrito) are linkage isomers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction N₂(g) + 3H₂(g) ⇌ 2NH₃(g), the equilibrium constant Kp is 1.6 × 10⁻⁴ atm⁻² at 400°C. The value of Kc at the same temperature is (R = 0.0821 L atm mol⁻¹ K⁻¹)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following ions has maximum magnetic moment?
questions.explanation: Magnetic moment μ = √[n(n+2)] BM. Mn²⁺ (d⁵) has 5 unpaired electrons, μ = √35 = 5.92 BM. Fe²⁺ (d⁶) has 4 unpaired, μ = √24 = 4.90 BM. Co²⁺ (d⁷) has 3 unpaired, μ = √15 = 3.87 BM. Ni²⁺ (d⁸) has 2 unpaired, μ = √8 = 2.83 BM.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed in the reaction of benzaldehyde with ammonia is
questions.explanation: Benzaldehyde reacts with ammonia to form hydrobenzamide (C₆H₅CH=N–CH(C₆H₅)–N=CHC₆H₅). Three molecules of benzaldehyde condense with two molecules of ammonia.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potential for the half-cell reactions are: Fe³⁺ + e⁻ → Fe²⁺, E° = +0.77 V Fe²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Fe, E° = –0.44 V The standard electrode potential for Fe³⁺ + 3e⁻ → Fe is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest boiling point among hydrides of group 16?
questions.explanation: H₂O has highest boiling point due to extensive intermolecular hydrogen bonding. Other hydrides have weaker van der Waals forces, boiling points increase down the group due to increasing molecular mass.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard free energy change for a reaction is –50 kJ/mol at 300 K. The equilibrium constant for the reaction is approximately (R = 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed in the reaction of 1,3-butadiene with maleic anhydride is
questions.explanation: 1,3-butadiene (diene) reacts with maleic anhydride (dienophile) in a Diels-Alder cycloaddition reaction to form a cyclic adduct (4-cyclohexene-1,2-dicarboxylic anhydride).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a copolymer?
questions.explanation: Buna-S is a copolymer of butadiene and styrene. Polythene, polypropylene, and PVC are homopolymers (made from single monomer type).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of CH₃–CH–CH=CH–CH₃ is | CH₃
questions.explanation: The longest chain has 5 carbons with double bond (pentene). Numbering should give double bond lowest number: double bond at C-2, methyl at C-4 gives name 4-methylpent-2-ene.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following alcohols will react fastest with Lucas reagent?
questions.explanation: Lucas reagent (HCl + ZnCl₂) reacts fastest with tertiary alcohols. (CH₃)₃COH is tertiary alcohol and gives immediate turbidity. Primary alcohols react slowly, secondary at intermediate rate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The coordination number of cobalt in [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]Cl is
questions.explanation: In [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]Cl, the ligands inside coordination sphere are 4 NH₃ and 2 Cl⁻, total 6 ligands. Coordination number is 6, oxidation state of Co is +3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is an essential amino acid?
questions.explanation: Essential amino acids cannot be synthesized by human body and must be obtained from diet. Lysine is essential. Glycine, alanine, and serine are non-essential.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for depletion of ozone layer?
questions.explanation: Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) release chlorine free radicals in stratosphere that catalytically destroy ozone molecules, leading to ozone layer depletion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The bond angle in ammonia molecule is approximately
questions.explanation: NH₃ has sp³ hybridization with one lone pair and three bond pairs. Lone pair-bond pair repulsion reduces bond angle from ideal 109.5° to 107.5°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Baking soda is chemically
questions.explanation: Baking soda is sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO₃). It is used in baking as leavening agent, releasing CO₂ when heated or reacted with acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest boiling point?
questions.explanation: Boiling point decreases with branching due to decreased surface area and van der Waals forces. n-pentane (straight chain) has highest boiling point, neopentane (highly branched) has lowest.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is not a characteristic of transition metals?
questions.explanation: Transition metals generally have high melting points due to strong metallic bonding involving unpaired d-electrons. Low melting points are not characteristic of transition metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a second order reaction, the unit of rate constant is
questions.explanation: For second order reaction, rate = k[A]². So k = rate/[A]² = (mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹)/(mol L⁻¹)² = L mol⁻¹ s⁻¹.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the most acidic hydrogen?
questions.explanation: Acidity ∝ s-character of carbon bonded to H. sp carbon (50% s) in alkynes is most electronegative, making acetylenic hydrogen most acidic. sp² (33%) in alkenes, sp³ (25%) in alkanes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will not give iodoform test?
questions.explanation: Iodoform test requires CH₃–CO– or CH₃–CH(OH)– group. Propanal (CH₃CH₂CHO) has no methyl group attached to carbonyl or carbinol carbon, so negative test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molarity of a solution containing 5.85 g of NaCl in 500 mL of solution is
questions.explanation: Molar mass NaCl = 58.5 g/mol. Moles = 5.85/58.5 = 0.1 mol. Volume = 500 mL = 0.5 L. Molarity = 0.1/0.5 = 0.2 M.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For which of the following processes will ΔS be positive?
questions.explanation: Entropy increases when number of gaseous molecules increases. In decomposition of CaCO₃, solid produces gas, randomness increases (ΔS > 0). Other reactions decrease number of gas molecules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is used in the manufacture of sulphuric acid?
questions.explanation: Vanadium pentoxide (V₂O₅) is used as catalyst in Contact process for oxidation of SO₂ to SO₃ in manufacture of sulphuric acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard electrode potential for Cu²⁺/Cu is +0.34 V and for Zn²⁺/Zn is –0.76 V. The standard emf for the cell Zn|Zn²⁺||Cu²⁺|Cu is
questions.explanation: E°cell = E°cathode – E°anode = E°Cu²⁺/Cu – E°Zn²⁺/Zn = 0.34 – (–0.76) = 1.10 V. The reaction is spontaneous with positive emf.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction 2NO₂(g) ⇌ N₂O₄(g), Kp = 8.0 at 27°C. The value of Kc at the same temperature is (R = 0.0821 L atm mol⁻¹ K⁻¹)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will give a precipitate with ammoniacal AgNO₃?
questions.explanation: Terminal alkynes (with ≡C–H) react with ammoniacal AgNO₃ to form white precipitate of silver acetylide. Internal alkynes do not give this test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: When phenol is treated with neutral FeCl₃, it gives
questions.explanation: Phenol gives violet colouration with neutral FeCl₃ due to formation of coloured complex. This is a characteristic test for phenolic –OH group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The reaction of aniline with chloroform and alcoholic KOH gives
questions.explanation: Carbylamine reaction: Primary aromatic amines react with CHCl₃ and alcoholic KOH to form foul-smelling isocyanides. Aniline gives phenyl isocyanide (C₆H₅NC).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest bond dissociation energy?
questions.explanation: Bond dissociation energy order: Cl₂ > Br₂ > F₂ > I₂. F₂ has low bond energy due to lone pair repulsion. Cl₂ has highest bond energy (242 kJ/mol) among halogens.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed when acetaldehyde is treated with dilute NaOH is
questions.explanation: Acetaldehyde undergoes aldol condensation with dilute NaOH to form 3-hydroxybutanal (aldol): 2CH₃CHO → CH₃–CH(OH)–CH₂–CHO.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is a fibre?
questions.explanation: Nylon-6,6 is a synthetic fibre (polyamide) used in textiles. Polythene, PVC, and teflon are plastics (thermoplastics) used for various applications.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The half-life of a first order reaction is 100 s. The time required for 75% completion of the reaction is
questions.explanation: For first order, time for 75% completion = 2 × t₁/₂ = 2 × 100 = 200 s. After two half-lives, 25% remains (75% completed).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will have the highest freezing point?
questions.explanation: Freezing point depression ∝ i × m. Glucose (i=1) has least depression, so highest freezing point. AlCl₃ (i=4) has lowest freezing point.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The magnetic moment of [Mn(CN)₆]³⁻ is
questions.explanation: In [Mn(CN)₆]³⁻, Mn is in +3 state (d⁴). CN⁻ is strong field ligand, causes pairing. Electronic configuration: t₂g⁴ (2 unpaired electrons). μ = √[2(2+2)] = √8 = 2.83 BM.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The heat of neutralization of a strong acid and strong base is

options.D: depends on the acid and base

questions.explanation: Neutralization is exothermic, ΔH is always negative. For strong acid-strong base, ΔH is constant –57.1 kJ/mol (heat released).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: During electrolysis of aqueous CuSO₄ using platinum electrodes, the products at anode and cathode are respectively
questions.explanation: At cathode: Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu (copper deposited). At anode: 2H₂O → O₂ + 4H⁺ + 4e⁻ (oxygen evolved). Copper is liberated at cathode, oxygen at anode.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following will not show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: For geometrical isomerism, each doubly bonded carbon must have two different groups. In CH₃–CH=CCl–CH₃, both carbons have different groups, so it should show isomerism. Question may have error.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The sugar present in DNA is
questions.explanation: DNA contains 2-deoxyribose sugar (lacks oxygen at C-2'). RNA contains ribose sugar (has –OH at C-2').

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The shape of XeF₂ molecule is
questions.explanation: XeF₂ has sp³d hybridization with three lone pairs and two bond pairs. The molecule is linear (bond angle 180°) with lone pairs in equatorial positions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The rate constant of a reaction increases with increase in temperature due to

options.C: increase in number of effective collisions

questions.explanation: According to Arrhenius theory, increase in temperature increases fraction of molecules with energy ≥ activation energy, thus increasing effective collisions and rate constant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following compounds will show optical isomerism?
questions.explanation: Optical isomerism requires chiral carbon (carbon with four different groups). In CH₃–CHBr–COOH, the central carbon has H, CH₃, Br, and COOH (four different groups), hence chiral.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction PCl₅(g) ⇌ PCl₃(g) + Cl₂(g), the degree of dissociation is α. The total pressure at equilibrium is P. The equilibrium constant Kp is given by
questions.explanation: Initial: 1 mole PCl₅. At equilibrium: (1–α) PCl₅, α PCl₃, α Cl₂. Total moles = 1+α. Partial pressures: P_PCl₅ = (1–α)P/(1+α), P_PCl₃ = αP/(1+α), P_Cl₂ = αP/(1+α). Kp = (α²P²/(1+α)²) / ((1–α)P/(1+α)) = α²P/(1–α²).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed in the reaction of acetone with ammonia is
questions.explanation: Acetone reacts with ammonia to form diacetone amine [(CH₃)₂C=NH–C(CH₃)=NH?] or more correctly, triacetoneamine (triacetonamine) through condensation reactions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard reduction potentials of Ag, Cu, Zn and Mg are +0.80, +0.34, –0.76 and –2.37 V respectively. Which metal is the strongest reducing agent?
questions.explanation: Reducing power ∝ 1/E°red. More negative E°red means stronger reducing agent. Mg has most negative E° (–2.37 V), hence strongest reducing agent.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the correct order of acidic strength of oxyacids of chlorine?
questions.explanation: Acidic strength increases with increasing oxidation state of Cl. Oxidation states: HClO (+1), HClO₂ (+3), HClO₃ (+5), HClO₄ (+7). Higher oxidation state stabilizes conjugate base.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction 2A(g) + B(g) → 3C(g), ΔU = –10 kJ and ΔS = –50 J/K at 300 K. The value of ΔG is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The product formed in the reaction of benzene with n-propyl chloride in presence of anhydrous AlCl₃ is
questions.explanation: In Friedel-Crafts alkylation, n-propyl carbocation rearranges to more stable isopropyl carbocation, giving isopropylbenzene (cumene) as major product.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the solubility of alcohols in water is correct?

options.A: All alcohols are infinitely soluble in water
options.B: Solubility of alcohols decreases with increase in molecular mass
options.C: Tertiary alcohols are more soluble than primary alcohols
options.D: Solubility of alcohols increases with increase in molecular mass

questions.explanation: Solubility of alcohols in water decreases with increase in molecular mass because the hydrocarbon part (hydrophobic) becomes larger and dominates over the hydrophilic -OH group. Option (a) is incorrect as higher alcohols are insoluble. Option (c) is incorrect - branching affects solubility but not in this simple order. Option (d) is opposite of correct trend.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the boiling points of organic compounds.

options.A: Boiling point increases with increase in branching
options.B: Boiling point of ethers is higher than corresponding alcohols
options.C: Boiling point of alcohols is higher than corresponding alkanes due to hydrogen bonding
options.D: Boiling point decreases with increase in molecular mass

questions.explanation: Alcohols have higher boiling points than corresponding alkanes due to intermolecular hydrogen bonding. Option (a) is false - branching decreases boiling point. Option (b) is false - ethers have lower boiling points than alcohols. Option (d) is false - boiling point increases with molecular mass generally.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about glucose is not correct?
questions.explanation: Glucose exhibits mutarotation due to existence of α and β anomers. Options (a), (b) and (c) are all correct statements about glucose.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the acidic strength of carboxylic acids, which one of the following is correct?

options.A: Acetic acid is stronger than formic acid
options.B: Chloroacetic acid is weaker than acetic acid
options.C: Trichloroacetic acid is stronger than acetic acid
options.D: Benzoic acid is stronger than formic acid

questions.explanation: Trichloroacetic acid is stronger than acetic acid due to -I effect of three Cl atoms. Option (a) is false - formic acid is stronger than acetic acid. Option (b) is false - chloroacetic acid is stronger due to -I effect. Option (d) is false - formic acid is stronger than benzoic acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about benzene is correct?

options.B: All carbon-carbon bonds in benzene are of equal length
options.C: Benzene has three single and three double bonds alternately
options.D: Benzene is more reactive than alkenes towards electrophilic addition

questions.explanation: Due to delocalization, all C-C bonds in benzene are of equal length (intermediate between single and double). Option (a) is false - benzene undergoes substitution, not addition. Option (c) is false - all bonds are identical due to resonance. Option (d) is false - benzene is less reactive than alkenes towards addition.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the reactivity of alkyl halides.

options.A: Tertiary alkyl halides are most reactive in SN2 reactions
options.B: Primary alkyl halides are most reactive in SN1 reactions
options.C: Allylic halides are more reactive than primary alkyl halides in SN1 reactions
options.D: Vinyl halides undergo SN2 reactions readily

questions.explanation: Allylic halides form stable allylic carbocation, hence more reactive in SN1. Option (a) is false - tertiary are least reactive in SN2. Option (b) is false - tertiary are most reactive in SN1. Option (d) is false - vinyl halides are unreactive in SN2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reaction of carbonyl compounds with HCN is correct?

options.D: Di-tert-butyl ketone reacts readily with HCN

questions.explanation: Formaldehyde reacts fastest with HCN due to least steric hindrance and high electrophilicity. Option (b) is false - benzaldehyde reacts faster than acetophenone. Option (c) is false - acetaldehyde reacts faster than acetone. Option (d) is false - highly hindered ketones do not react readily.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the basicity of amines in aqueous solution, which statement is correct?

options.A: All amines are stronger bases than ammonia
options.B: Aniline is stronger base than methylamine
options.C: Dimethylamine is stronger base than trimethylamine
options.D: p-Nitroaniline is stronger base than aniline

questions.explanation: In aqueous solution, dimethylamine is stronger base than trimethylamine due to solvation effects. Option (a) is false - arylamines are weaker than ammonia. Option (b) is false - methylamine is stronger than aniline. Option (d) is false - p-nitroaniline is weaker due to -NO₂ group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the stability of carbocations is correct?

options.B: Primary carbocation is more stable than secondary
options.C: Tertiary carbocation is more stable than secondary due to hyperconjugation
options.D: Vinyl carbocation is more stable than ethyl carbocation

questions.explanation: Tertiary carbocation is most stable due to maximum hyperconjugation and inductive effect. Option (a) is false - methyl is least stable. Option (b) is false - secondary > primary. Option (d) is false - vinyl carbocation is highly unstable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the dipole moment of molecules.

options.A: CO₂ has a high dipole moment
options.B: CH₄ has a significant dipole moment
options.D: NF₃ has lower dipole moment than NH₃

questions.explanation: NF₃ has lower dipole moment than NH₃ because in NF₃, the net bond dipole opposes the lone pair dipole. Option (a) is false - CO₂ is linear and non-polar. Option (b) is false - CH₄ is symmetrical with zero dipole. Option (c) is false - NH₃ has significant dipole moment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the solubility of ionic compounds is correct?

options.A: All sodium salts are insoluble in water
options.B: All nitrates are soluble in water
options.C: All carbonates are soluble in water
options.D: All chlorides are insoluble in water

questions.explanation: All nitrates are soluble in water. Option (a) is false - most sodium salts are soluble. Option (c) is false - most carbonates are insoluble except those of alkali metals. Option (d) is false - most chlorides are soluble except AgCl, PbCl₂, etc.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the hydrolysis of salts, which statement is correct?
questions.explanation: CH₃COONH₄ is salt of weak acid and weak base with similar Ka and Kb, hence approximately neutral. Option (a) is false - NaCl is neutral. Option (b) is false - CH₃COONa is basic. Option (c) is false - NH₄Cl is acidic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the properties of phenol is correct?

options.A: Phenol is more acidic than ethanol
options.B: Phenol is less acidic than water
options.C: Phenol does not react with NaOH
