options.D: Phenol gives white precipitate with Br₂ water

questions.explanation: Phenol is more acidic than ethanol due to resonance stabilization of phenoxide ion. Option (b) is false - phenol is more acidic than water. Option (c) is false - phenol reacts with NaOH. Option (d) is false - phenol gives 2,4,6-tribromophenol which is pale yellow, not white.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the reactions of aldehydes and ketones.

options.A: Both aldehydes and ketones give positive Tollen's test
options.B: Aldehydes are more reactive than ketones towards nucleophilic addition
options.D: Formaldehyde does not give Fehling's test

questions.explanation: Aldehydes are more reactive than ketones due to less steric hindrance and greater electrophilicity. Option (a) is false - ketones do not give Tollen's test. Option (c) is false - only aldehydes without α-hydrogen undergo Cannizzaro. Option (d) is false - formaldehyde gives Fehling's test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the structure of benzene is correct according to molecular orbital theory?

options.A: Benzene has three localized double bonds
options.B: All carbon atoms in benzene are sp³ hybridized
options.C: The π-electrons are delocalized over all six carbon atoms
options.D: Benzene has alternating long and short bonds

questions.explanation: According to MO theory, the π-electrons in benzene are completely delocalized over all six carbon atoms. Option (a) is false - bonds are delocalized. Option (b) is false - carbons are sp² hybridized. Option (d) is false - all bond lengths are equal.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the acidic strength of oxyacids of chlorine, which statement is correct?
questions.explanation: HClO₄ is the strongest oxyacid of chlorine due to maximum oxidation state of Cl (+7). Option (a) is false - HOCl is weakest. Option (b) is false - HClO₂ is stronger than HOCl. Option (c) is false - HClO₄ is stronger than HClO₃.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reactivity of halogens is correct?

options.A: Fluorine is the least reactive halogen
options.B: Chlorine can displace bromine from bromide solution
options.C: Iodine can displace chlorine from chloride solution
options.D: Bromine cannot displace iodine from iodide solution

questions.explanation: Chlorine being more reactive can displace bromine from bromide solution. Option (a) is false - fluorine is most reactive. Option (c) is false - iodine is least reactive, cannot displace chlorine. Option (d) is false - bromine can displace iodine.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the properties of transition elements.

options.B: Transition elements show variable oxidation states
options.D: Transition elements are poor conductors of electricity

questions.explanation: Transition elements show variable oxidation states due to involvement of (n-1)d electrons. Option (a) is false - many are paramagnetic. Option (c) is false - they form coloured compounds. Option (d) is false - they are good conductors.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the lanthanoids is correct?

options.B: Lanthanoid contraction is due to poor shielding of 4f electrons
options.D: Lanthanoids show maximum oxidation state of +8

questions.explanation: Lanthanoid contraction is due to poor shielding of 4f electrons. Option (a) is false - most show +3, some show +2 and +4. Option (c) is false - only promethium is radioactive. Option (d) is false - maximum is +4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the bond angles in different molecules, which statement is correct?

options.B: Bond angle in NH₃ is greater than in CH₄

questions.explanation: CO₂ is linear with bond angle 180°. Option (a) is false - H₂O is 104.5°. Option (b) is false - CH₄ (109.5°) > NH₃ (107°). Option (d) is false - CH₄ is 109.5°.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the preparation of alkenes is correct?

options.A: Dehydration of alcohols requires concentrated H₂SO₄ at room temperature
options.C: Vicinal dibromides on treatment with Zn give alkynes

questions.explanation: Dehydrohalogenation of alkyl halides requires alcoholic KOH. Option (a) is false - requires heating. Option (c) is false - gives alkenes, not alkynes. Option (d) is false - gives alkanes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the distinction between primary, secondary and tertiary alcohols.

options.A: All alcohols give turbidity immediately with Lucas reagent
options.B: Tertiary alcohols give turbidity immediately with Lucas reagent
options.C: Lucas reagent is a mixture of HCl and HNO₃
options.D: Primary alcohols react fastest with Lucas reagent

questions.explanation: Tertiary alcohols react fastest with Lucas reagent (anhydrous ZnCl₂ + conc. HCl) and give turbidity immediately. Option (a) is false - only tertiary give immediate turbidity. Option (c) is false - Lucas reagent is HCl + ZnCl₂. Option (d) is false - primary react slowest.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reaction of carboxylic acids is correct?

options.A: Carboxylic acids do not react with alcohols
options.B: Carboxylic acids react with PCl₅ to form acid chlorides
options.C: Carboxylic acids on reduction give aldehydes directly
options.D: Carboxylic acids give CO₂ with NaHCO₃ only on heating

questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids react with PCl₅ to form acid chlorides. Option (a) is false - they undergo esterification. Option (c) is false - reduction gives primary alcohols. Option (d) is false - they give CO₂ at room temperature with NaHCO₃.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the basicity of amines, which statement is correct?

options.A: Aliphatic amines are weaker bases than ammonia
options.B: Aromatic amines are stronger bases than aliphatic amines
options.C: Aniline is weaker base than ammonia
options.D: p-Toluidine is weaker base than aniline

questions.explanation: Aniline is weaker base than ammonia due to delocalization of lone pair into benzene ring. Option (a) is false - aliphatic amines are stronger than ammonia. Option (b) is false - aromatic amines are weaker. Option (d) is false - p-toluidine is stronger due to +I effect of CH₃.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reactivity of carbonyl compounds is correct?

options.A: Formaldehyde is less reactive than acetaldehyde towards nucleophilic addition
options.B: Benzaldehyde is more reactive than formaldehyde
options.C: Acetone is more reactive than acetaldehyde
options.D: Chloral (CCl₃CHO) is more reactive than acetaldehyde

questions.explanation: Chloral is more reactive due to strong -I effect of CCl₃ group. Option (a) is false - formaldehyde is most reactive. Option (b) is false - formaldehyde is more reactive. Option (c) is false - acetaldehyde is more reactive than acetone.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the stability of conformations is correct?

options.A: Eclipsed conformation of ethane is most stable
options.B: Staggered conformation of ethane has maximum potential energy
options.C: Gauche conformation of n-butane is more stable than anti conformation
options.D: Anti conformation of n-butane is most stable

questions.explanation: Anti conformation of n-butane is most stable due to minimum steric repulsion between bulky CH₃ groups. Option (a) is false - staggered is most stable. Option (b) is false - staggered has minimum energy. Option (c) is false - anti is more stable than gauche.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the acidic strength of phenols, which statement is correct?

options.A: Phenol is stronger acid than p-nitrophenol
options.B: o-Nitrophenol is stronger acid than phenol
options.C: p-Cresol is stronger acid than phenol
options.D: Picric acid is weaker acid than phenol

questions.explanation: o-Nitrophenol is stronger than phenol due to -I and -M effect of NO₂ group. Option (a) is false - p-nitrophenol is stronger. Option (c) is false - p-cresol is weaker due to +I effect. Option (d) is false - picric acid is much stronger.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reactions of alkynes is correct?

options.A: Alkynes do not react with hydrogen
options.B: Terminal alkynes react with ammoniacal AgNO₃ to give white precipitate
options.C: All alkynes give precipitate with ammoniacal AgNO₃
options.D: Alkynes on partial reduction with Lindlar's catalyst give trans-alkenes

questions.explanation: Terminal alkynes react with ammoniacal AgNO₃ to form white precipitate of silver acetylide. Option (a) is false - they undergo hydrogenation. Option (c) is false - only terminal alkynes react. Option (d) is false - Lindlar's catalyst gives cis-alkenes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the preparation of amines.

options.A: Gabriel phthalimide synthesis is used for preparing aromatic primary amines
options.B: Reduction of nitrobenzene with Sn/HCl gives aniline
options.C: Hoffmann bromamide reaction converts amides to amines with same number of carbons
options.D: Carbylamine reaction is given by all amines

questions.explanation: Reduction of nitrobenzene with Sn/HCl gives aniline. Option (a) is false - used for aliphatic primary amines. Option (c) is false - gives amine with one carbon less. Option (d) is false - only primary amines give this reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the hybridization of carbon atoms is correct?

options.A: Carbon in CH₄ is sp² hybridized
options.B: Carbon in C₂H₄ is sp³ hybridized
options.C: Carbon in C₂H₂ is sp hybridized
options.D: Carbon in CO₂ is sp³ hybridized

questions.explanation: Carbon in acetylene (C₂H₂) is sp hybridized with bond angle 180°. Option (a) is false - CH₄ is sp³. Option (b) is false - C₂H₄ is sp². Option (d) is false - CO₂ is sp.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the properties of colloids, which statement is correct?

options.A: Colloidal particles are visible to naked eye
options.C: Colloidal particles settle down on standing

questions.explanation: Colloidal solutions show Tyndall effect due to scattering of light by colloidal particles. Option (a) is false - particles are not visible. Option (c) is false - they are stable and don't settle. Option (d) is false - they are heterogeneous.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reaction of alkyl halides is correct?

options.A: Primary alkyl halides undergo SN1 reaction readily
options.B: Tertiary alkyl halides undergo SN2 reaction readily
options.C: Allylic halides are more reactive than vinyl halides in SN2 reactions
options.D: Vinyl halides undergo SN2 reactions as readily as alkyl halides

questions.explanation: Allylic halides are more reactive than vinyl halides due to resonance stabilization of transition state. Option (a) is false - primary favor SN2. Option (b) is false - tertiary favor SN1. Option (d) is false - vinyl halides are unreactive.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the electrochemical series.

options.A: Lithium has the highest standard reduction potential
options.B: Fluorine has the highest standard reduction potential
options.C: Gold has the lowest standard reduction potential
options.D: Sodium has higher reduction potential than lithium

questions.explanation: Fluorine has the highest standard reduction potential (+2.87 V) as it is the strongest oxidizing agent. Option (a) is false - Li has low reduction potential (-3.05 V). Option (c) is false - Au has high reduction potential. Option (d) is false - Na has lower reduction potential than Li? Actually Li has -3.05, Na has -2.71, so Li is lower.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reactions of benzene is correct?

options.B: Nitration of benzene requires fuming HNO₃ alone
options.D: Benzene does not undergo electrophilic substitution

questions.explanation: Friedel-Crafts reaction requires anhydrous AlCl₃ as catalyst. Option (a) is false - benzene undergoes substitution. Option (b) is false - requires conc. H₂SO₄ also. Option (d) is false - benzene undergoes electrophilic substitution readily.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the properties of d-block elements, which statement is correct?

options.B: d-block elements show catalytic activity due to variable oxidation states
options.C: d-block elements form only ionic compounds

questions.explanation: d-block elements show catalytic activity due to variable oxidation states and ability to form complexes. Option (a) is false - they are metals. Option (c) is false - they form both ionic and covalent compounds. Option (d) is false - many are paramagnetic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reaction of carbonyl compounds with ammonia derivatives is correct?

options.A: Aldehydes react with hydroxylamine to form hydrazones
options.B: Ketones react with hydrazine to form oximes
options.C: Aldehydes react with semicarbazide to form semicarbazones
options.D: All carbonyl compounds give same products with all ammonia derivatives

questions.explanation: Aldehydes and ketones react with semicarbazide to form semicarbazones. Option (a) is false - they form oximes. Option (b) is false - they form hydrazones. Option (d) is false - products depend on the specific reagent.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the hydrogen bonding.

options.A: Hydrogen bonding is strongest in H₂S
options.B: Hydrogen bonding is absent in HF
options.C: Hydrogen bonding is present in CH₄
options.D: Hydrogen bonding is responsible for high boiling point of water

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is responsible for the high boiling point of water. Option (a) is false - strongest in HF. Option (b) is false - HF shows strong H-bonding. Option (c) is false - CH₄ has no H-bonding.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reactions of carboxylic acid derivatives is correct?

options.A: Acid chlorides are least reactive towards nucleophilic substitution
options.B: Amides are most reactive towards nucleophilic substitution
options.C: Esters are more reactive than acid chlorides
options.D: Acid anhydrides are more reactive than amides

questions.explanation: Acid anhydrides are more reactive than amides towards nucleophilic substitution. Order of reactivity: Acid chlorides > Acid anhydrides > Esters > Amides. Options (a), (b), (c) are all opposite to this order.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the structure of proteins, which statement is correct?

options.A: Proteins are composed of only one type of amino acid
options.B: Primary structure refers to the three-dimensional arrangement
options.C: Peptide bond has partial double bond character
options.D: α-Helix is an example of quaternary structure

questions.explanation: Peptide bond has partial double bond character due to resonance, making it planar. Option (a) is false - proteins contain multiple amino acids. Option (b) is false - primary structure is sequence of amino acids. Option (d) is false - α-helix is secondary structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the reactivity of metals is correct?

options.A: Potassium reacts less vigorously with water than sodium
options.B: Calcium reacts with water at room temperature
options.C: Magnesium does not react with steam
options.D: Aluminum reacts vigorously with cold water

questions.explanation: Calcium reacts with water at room temperature. Option (a) is false - K reacts more vigorously. Option (c) is false - Mg reacts with steam. Option (d) is false - Al forms protective oxide layer, reacts slowly.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the electrolysis.

options.A: During electrolysis, oxidation occurs at cathode
options.B: During electrolysis, reduction occurs at anode
options.C: During electrolysis of NaCl solution, H₂ is liberated at cathode
options.D: During electrolysis of CuSO₄ with Cu electrodes, Cu is deposited at anode

questions.explanation: During electrolysis of NaCl solution, H₂ is liberated at cathode due to lower reduction potential of H₂O. Option (a) is false - oxidation occurs at anode. Option (b) is false - reduction occurs at cathode. Option (d) is false - Cu is deposited at cathode, anode dissolves.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the periodic trends is correct?

options.A: Atomic radius increases across a period
options.B: Ionization energy decreases across a period
options.D: Metallic character increases across a period

questions.explanation: Electronegativity increases across a period from left to right. Option (a) is false - atomic radius decreases. Option (b) is false - ionization energy increases. Option (d) is false - metallic character decreases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the reaction mechanisms, which statement is correct?

options.A: SN1 reactions proceed with inversion of configuration
options.C: E2 reactions require strong base and proceed through carbocation
options.D: E1 reactions proceed through carbocation intermediate

questions.explanation: E1 reactions proceed through carbocation intermediate in two steps. Option (a) is false - SN1 gives racemization. Option (b) is false - SN2 gives inversion. Option (c) is false - E2 is concerted, no carbocation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the qualitative analysis of organic compounds is correct?

options.B: Sodium fusion extract is used for detection of halogens only
options.C: Prussian blue color confirms presence of nitrogen
options.D: Silver nitrate test is used for detection of sulphur

questions.explanation: Prussian blue color formation confirms presence of nitrogen in Lassaigne's test. Option (a) is false - detects S, halogens also. Option (b) is false - used for N, S, halogens. Option (d) is false - used for halogens.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the isotopes.

options.A: All isotopes of an element have same number of neutrons
options.B: All isotopes of an element have same atomic mass
options.C: All isotopes of an element have same chemical properties

questions.explanation: Isotopes have same electronic configuration, hence same chemical properties. Option (a) is false - they have same protons, different neutrons. Option (b) is false - atomic mass differs. Option (d) is false - many are stable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Regarding the properties of polymers, which statement is correct?

options.A: Natural rubber is a polymer of chloroprene

questions.explanation: Bakelite is a thermosetting polymer (phenol-formaldehyde resin). Option (a) is false - natural rubber is polymer of isoprene. Option (b) is false - Teflon is addition polymer. Option (c) is false - Nylon-66 is condensation polymer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the coordination compounds is correct?

options.C: Coordination number of central metal ion is fixed for a given metal
options.D: Ambidentate ligands can coordinate through different atoms

questions.explanation: Ambidentate ligands like NO₂⁻, SCN⁻ can coordinate through different donor atoms. Option (a) is false - some are non-electrolytes. Option (b) is false - ligands can be neutral also. Option (c) is false - coordination number varies.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding the environmental chemistry.

options.A: Ozone layer is beneficial as it absorbs infrared radiation
options.B: Carbon monoxide is a major greenhouse gas
options.C: Acid rain is caused by oxides of nitrogen and sulphur
options.D: BOD is a measure of oxygen dissolved in water

questions.explanation: Acid rain is caused by oxides of nitrogen and sulphur (SO₂, NOₓ). Option (a) is false - ozone absorbs UV radiation. Option (b) is false - CO₂, CH₄ are major greenhouse gases. Option (d) is false - BOD measures oxygen required by microorganisms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their bond angles and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Bond angles: H₂O (104.5°), NH₃ (107°), CH₄ (109.5°), CO₂ (180°). Increasing order: H₂O < NH₃ < CH₄ < CO₂ ⇒ I < II < III < IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their acidic strength and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Acidic strength: Picric acid (2,4,6-trinitrophenol) > p-Nitrophenol > Phenol > p-Cresol. Decreasing order: IV > II > I > III.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their boiling points and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Boiling points of alkanes increase with increase in molecular mass. Methane (-162°C), Ethane (-89°C), Propane (-42°C), Butane (-0.5°C). Increasing order: I < II < III < IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their reactivity towards electrophilic substitution and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Reactivity towards electrophilic substitution: Phenol (activating, -OH) > Toluene (activating, -CH₃) > Benzene > Nitrobenzene (deactivating). Decreasing order: II > IV > I > III.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their pKb values and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: pKb is inversely related to basic strength. In aqueous solution, basic strength order: (CH₃)₂NH > CH₃NH₂ > (CH₃)₃N > NH₃. So pKb order: (CH₃)₂NH < CH₃NH₂ < (CH₃)₃N < NH₃ ⇒ III < II < IV < I.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their bond dissociation enthalpy and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Bond dissociation enthalpy order: Cl₂ (242 kJ/mol) > Br₂ (192) > F₂ (158) > I₂ (151). F₂ has lower value due to electron-electron repulsion. Decreasing order: II > III > I > IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their ionisation enthalpy and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Ionisation enthalpy order: Na (496) < Al (577) < Mg (737) < Si (786). Mg has higher than Al due to stable s² configuration. Increasing order: I < III < II < IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their thermal stability and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Thermal stability of carbonates increases down the group: BeCO₃ < MgCO₃ < CaCO₃ < SrCO₃. Decreasing order: SrCO₃ > CaCO₃ > MgCO₃ > BeCO₃ ⇒ IV > I > II > III.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their solubility in water and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Solubility of alcohols in water decreases as molecular mass increases. Methanol > Ethanol > Propanol > Butanol. Increasing order: Butanol < Propanol < Ethanol < Methanol ⇒ I < II < III < IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their melting points and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Melting points: Diamond (>3500°C) > NaCl (801°C) > Graphite (3650°C? Actually graphite sublimes around 3650°C, but NaCl is ionic with high MP) Wait order: Diamond (~4000°C) > Graphite (~3650°C) > NaCl (801°C) > Naphthalene (80°C). So I > II > III > IV is correct. But option (a) is I > II > III > IV, (b) is I > III > II > IV. Diamond has highest, then graphite, then NaCl, then naphthalene. So (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their lattice energy and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Lattice energy depends on charge and size. MgO (Mg²⁺, O²⁻) highest, then CaO (Ca²⁺, O²⁻), then NaF (Na⁺, F⁻), then NaCl (Na⁺, Cl⁻). Increasing order: NaCl < NaF < CaO < MgO ⇒ I < IV < III < II.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their reactivity towards SN2 reaction and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: SN2 reactivity decreases with increase in steric hindrance. Order: CH₃-Br > CH₃CH₂-Br > (CH₃)₂CH-Br > (CH₃)₃C-Br. Decreasing order: I > II > III > IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their covalent character and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Covalent character increases as size of cation decreases (Fajan's rule). Order: RbCl < KCl < NaCl < LiCl. Increasing order: IV < III < II < I.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their electron affinity and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Electron affinity order: Cl (349) > F (328) > Br (325) > I (295). Decreasing order: I > II > III > IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their pKa values and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: pKa is inversely related to acid strength. Acid strength order: Trichloroacetic acid > Chloroacetic acid > Formic acid > Acetic acid. So pKa order: Trichloroacetic < Chloroacetic < Formic < Acetic ⇒ III < II < IV < I.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their boiling points and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Boiling points: n-Hexane (69°C) > n-Pentane (36°C) > Isopentane (28°C) > Neopentane (9.5°C). Decreasing order: IV > I > II > III.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their ionic radii and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: For isoelectronic species (10 electrons), ionic radius ∝ 1/charge. Order of increasing radius: Al³⁺ (charge +3) < Mg²⁺ (+2) < Na⁺ (+1) < F⁻ (-1). So III < II < I < IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their dipole moment and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Dipole moments: H₂O (1.85 D) > NH₃ (1.47 D) > NF₃ (0.24 D) > BF₃ (0 D). Decreasing order: I > II > III > IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their hydration energy and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Hydration energy decreases as ionic size increases. Order: Li⁺ > Na⁺ > K⁺ > Rb⁺. Increasing order of hydration energy would be reverse: Rb⁺ < K⁺ < Na⁺ < Li⁺ ⇒ IV < III < II < I.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their reducing character and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Reducing character of hydrogen halides increases down the group as bond strength decreases. Order: HI > HBr > HCl > HF. Decreasing order: IV > III > II > I.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their standard electrode potential and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Standard electrode potentials: Li⁺/Li (-3.05 V), Na⁺/Na (-2.71 V), K⁺/K (-2.93 V), Rb⁺/Rb (-2.98 V). Actually order is Li (-3.05) < K (-2.93) < Rb (-2.98) < Na (-2.71)? This is confusing. Based on typical order, Li has most negative, then K, then Rb, then Na. So I < III < IV < II. But option (a) is I < II < III < IV which is not correct. Option (a) might be intended as increasing (more negative to less negative): Li (-3.05) < K (-2.93) < Rb (-2.98) < Na (-2.71) is not strictly increasing. There might be error in options.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their oxidizing power and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Oxidizing power of halogens decreases down the group. F₂ is strongest oxidizer, then Cl₂, then Br₂, then I₂. Decreasing order: I > II > III > IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their van der Waals radius and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: van der Waals radius increases down the group: He < Ne < Ar < Kr. Increasing order: I < II < III < IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their density and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Densities (g/cm³): Osmium (22.6) > Platinum (21.5) > Iron (7.9) > Aluminum (2.7). Decreasing order: I > II > III > IV.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Arrange the following in increasing order of their solubility product (Ksp) and select the correct sequence.
questions.explanation: Ksp values: Ag₂S (~6×10⁻⁵⁰) < AgI (8.5×10⁻¹⁷) < AgBr (5×10⁻¹³) < AgCl (1.8×10⁻¹⁰). Increasing order: Ag₂S < AgI < AgBr < AgCl ⇒ IV < III < II < I.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Bond dissociation energy of F₂ is less than that of Cl₂.
Reason (R): F₂ has strong lone pair-lone pair repulsion due to small size.

options.A: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
options.B: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A)

questions.explanation: F₂ has unexpectedly low bond dissociation energy due to strong electron-electron repulsion between lone pairs on adjacent small F atoms. Cl₂ has larger size, less repulsion, higher bond energy. Reason correctly explains.
